Payments
POST /standard/v3/disbursements

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
X-Country	header	string	true	Country of transaction.
X-Currency	header	string	true	Currency of transaction.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization Bearer Token.
x-signature	header	string	false	Encrypted payload.
x-key	header	string	false	Encrypted AES key and iv
Request Body
Attribute	Description
payee
object
mandatory	payee
payee.msisdn
string
mandatory	MSISDN without the country code of the payee to which the payment was credited.
payee.wallet_type
string
mandatory	Type of wallet where the payee wants the money to be credited.
reference
string
mandatory	Reference for service / goods purchased.
pin
string
mandatory	Encrypted four digit numeric pin to be send in a transaction request to complete the payment. To check for encryption Please click here
transaction
object
mandatory	transaction
transaction.amount
number
mandatory	Transaction amount which will be credited to payee's wallet.
transaction.id
string
mandatory	Payer unique transaction id to identify the transaction.
transaction.type
string
mandatory	Transaction type
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
data
data.transaction
object
Disbursement credit transaction.
transaction.reference_id
string
Transaction id generated by payment service.
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM.
transaction.id
string
Transaction id generated by partner.
transaction.status
string
Status of the Transaction.
transaction.message
string
The descriptive message of the transaction.
status
object
status
status.response_code
string
Product specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.code
string
Status code, HTTP status code.
status.success
boolean
True if no error else false.
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.                   
                        curl -X POST https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/standard/v3/disbursements
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'Accept: */*'
  -H 'X-Country: CG'
  -H 'X-Currency: XAF'
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer UCcp1oe*******xCzki2w'
  -H 'x-signature: MGsp*********Ag=='
  -H 'x-key: DVZC*******************NM='

    "payee": {
        "msisdn": "75****26",
        "wallet_type": "SALARY"
    },
    "reference": "AB***141",
    "pin": "KYJ************Rsa44",
    "transaction": {
        "amount": 1000,
        "id": "AB***141",
        "type": "B2B"
    }
}

        "response_code": "DP00900001001",

Transaction Enquiry
This api gets the status for external transaction Id.

GET /standard/v3/disbursements/{id}

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
id	path	string	true	Transaction id.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
X-Country	header	string	true	Country of transaction.
X-Currency	header	string	true	Currency of transaction.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
data
data.transaction
object
Disbursement credit transaction.
transaction.reference_id
string
Transaction id generated by payment service.
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM.
transaction.id
string
Transaction id generated by partner.
transaction.status
string
Status of the Transaction
transaction.message
string
The descriptive message of the transaction.
status
object
Status Response.
status.response_code
string
Product specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.code
string
Status code, HTTP status code.
status.success
boolean
True if no error else false.
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.

            "status": "TS",
            "message": "Transaction Successful"
        }
    },
    "status": {
        "response_code": "DP00900001001",
        "code": "200",
        "success": true,
        "message": "SUCCESS"
    }
}
                       
                    URL obj = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/standard/v3/disbursements/{id}");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
  new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
  response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

                    <?php
require 'vendor/autoload.php';
$headers = array(
  'Accept' => '*/*',
  'X-Country' => 'CG',
  'X-Currency' => 'XAF',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UCcp1oe*******xCzki2w'
);
$client = new GuzzleHttpClient();
// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('GET', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/standard/v3/disbursements/{id}', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

                Disbursement-APIs
Version
3.0
Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean.\nThis page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Disbursement API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team.
Error Code	Expected Error Reason	Description
DP00900001000
Ambiguous	The transaction is still processing and is in ambiguous state. Please do the transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP00900001001
Success	Transaction is successful.
DP00900001003
Maximum transaction limit reached	Maximum transaction limit reached for the day.
DP00900001004
Invalid Amount	Amount entered is out of range with respect to defined limits.
DP00900001005
Failed	Transaction failed or refused.
DP00900001006
In progress	The transaction is still in progress. Please do the transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP00900001007
Insufficient Funds	Not enough funds in account to complete the transaction.
DP00900001009
Invalid Initiatee	Initiatee of the transaction is invalid.
DP00900001010
User Not Allowed	Payer is not an allowed user.
DP00900001012
Invalid Mobile Numer	Mobile number entered is incorrect
DP00900001013
Transaction Refused	The transaction was refused.
DP00900001015
Transaction not Found	Transaction is not found
DP00900001017
Duplicate Transaction Id	Duplicate Transaction Id.
DP00900001018
Forbidden	X-signature and payload did not match.
DP00900001019
Sender is Barred	Payer is Barred
Cash-In-APIs
Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Payments
This API is used to credits the amount to payee wallet from payer wallet.

POST /standard/v1/cashin/

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
X-Country	header	string	true	Country of transaction.
X-Currency	header	string	true	Currency of transaction.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization Bearer Token.
Request Body
Attribute	Description
subscriber
object
mandatory	Subscriber.Cash-In-APIs
Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Transaction Enquiry
This API gets the transaction status corresponding to the requested External Id.

GET /standard/v1/payments/{id}

Note : airtel_money_id is not generated in case of TIP (Transaction in Progress) and TF (Transaction Failed).This is only generated in case of successful transaction TS(Transaction Success). It is recommended to conduct a transaction inquiry at least one minute after the payment API has been called.
Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
id	path	string	true	Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
X-Country	header	string	true	Transaction country.
X-Currency	header	string	true	Transaction currency.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization Bearer Token
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
Transaction enquiry response.
data.transaction
object
Transaction response.
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM in case of successful transaction TS (Transaction Success).
transaction.id
string
Transaction id generated by TPP / Aggregator / Merchant.
transaction.status
string
Possible Status: TF (Transaction Failed) TS (Transaction Success) TA (Transaction Ambiguous) TIP (Transaction in Progress).
status
object
Status response.
status.code
string
Status code, HTTP status code.
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action.
status.result_code
string
Application specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.success
boolean
true if no error else false.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.Cash-In-APIs
Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Cash-In API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team
Error Code	Expected Error Reason	Description
DP01000001000
Ambiguous	The transaction is still processing and is in ambiguous state. Please do the transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP01000001001
Success	Transaction is successful.
DP01000001002
Incorrect Pin	Incorrect pin has been entered.
DP01000001003
Exceeds withdrawal amount limit(s) / Withdrawal amount limit exceeded	The User has exceeded their wallet allowed transaction limit.
DP01000001004
Invalid Amount	The amount user is trying to transfer is less than the minimum amount allowed.
DP01000001005
Failed	Transaction failed.
DP01000001006
In Process	Transaction in pending state. Please check after sometime.
DP01000001007
Insufficient Funds	User wallet does not have enough money to cover the payable amount.
DP01000001008
User Not Allowed	User is not allowed as a payer.
DP01000001009
Initiatee Invalid	Initiatee is invalid.
DP01000001010
Not Permitted	User not allowed as payer.
DP01000001011
Do Not Honour	Transaction is already completed.
DP01000001012
Invalid Mobile Number	Mobile number entered is incorrect or not registered.
DP01000001013
Failed / Refused	The transaction was either failed or refused.
DP01000001014
Transaction Timed Out	The transaction may be processed or failed due to time out. To know the exact status please do the transaction enquiry.ATM Withdrawal API
Version
2.0
Always stay ahead with the latest version! Older versions may be deprecated at any time, so for the best experience and support, we recommend using the most up-to-date API. Keep your integration future-proof!

Version Highlights:

1. Message signing feature has been introduced in this version for ATM Withdrawl Payment and Refund api. 2. Message signing feature can be enabled or disabled for cashin from the security section of the setting tab in application where ATM Withdrawl api is subscribed and approved.

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Payments
Debit the amount from payer wallet and credit in payee wallet.

POST /atmwithdrawal/v2/payments/

Note: authentication_medium can only be PAYCODE. payer.transfer_type can only be WALLET.
Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
x-country-code	header	string	true	Country of transaction.
x-currency-code	header	string	true	Currency of transaction.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
x-signature	header	string	true	Encrypted payload.
x-key	header	string	true	Encrypted AES key and iv.
Request Body
Attribute	Description
payer
object
mandatory	Withdrawal payer.
payer.transfer_type
string
mandatory	Payer transaction transfer type.
payer.msisdn
string
mandatory	MSISDN without the country code of the payer from which the payment will be debited.
payer.wallet_id
string
optional	wallet Id of the payer from which the payment will be debited.
payer.pay_code
string
mandatory	Pay code.
reference
string
optional	Reference for service / goods purchased.
transaction
object
mandatory	Withdrawal transaction detail.
transaction.amount
string
mandatory	Transaction amount which will be credited to payee's wallet.
transaction.id
string
mandatory	Payer unique transaction id to identify the transaction.
authentication_medium
string
mandatory	Channel type of the transaction.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
Atm withdrawal push response.
data.transaction
object
Transaction.
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM.
transaction.id
string
Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction.
transaction.reference_id
string
Reference for the transaction.
transaction.status
string
Transaction status. Possible values TS, TF, and TIP.
transaction.message
string
Message.
status
object
Status response.
status.code
string
Status code, HTTP status code.
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action.
status.response_code
string
Product specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.success
boolean
true if no error else false.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.Code samples

                    
                        curl -X POST https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/atmwithdrawal/v2/payments/
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'Accept: */*'
  -H 'x-country-code: CG'
  -H 'x-currency-code: XAF'
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer UC*****2w'
  -H 'x-signature: MGsp*********Ag=='
  -H 'x-key: DVZC***********NM='

Version
2.0
Always stay ahead with the latest version! Older versions may be deprecated at any time, so for the best experience and support, we recommend using the most up-to-date API. Keep your integration future-proof!

Version Highlights:

1. Message signing feature has been introduced in this version for ATM Withdrawl Payment and Refund api. 2. Message signing feature can be enabled or disabled for cashin from the security section of the setting tab in application where ATM Withdrawl api is subscribed and approved.

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Refund
Refund the amount from payee wallet and credit in payer wallet.

POST /atmwithdrawal/v2/payments/refund/

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
x-country-code	header	string	true	Country of transaction.
x-currency-code	header	string	true	Currency of transaction.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization Bearer token
x-signature	header	string	true	Encrypted payload.
x-key	header	string	true	Encrypted AES key and iv.
Request Body
Attribute	Description
transaction
object
mandatory	Transaction.
transaction.id
string
mandatory	Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
Payment refund response.
data.transaction
object
Refund transaction.
transaction.reference_id
string
Reference for the transaction.
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM.
transaction.id
string
Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction.
transaction.message
string
Message.
transaction.status
string
Transaction status. Possible values TS, TF, and TIP.
status
object
Status response.
status.response_code
string
Application specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.code
string
Status code, HTTP status code.
status.success
boolean
true if no error else false.
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.

    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

}ATM Withdrawal API
Version
2.0
Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the ATM Withdrawl API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team.
Error Code	Expected Error Reason	Description
DP08700001000
Ambiguous	The transaction is still processing and is in ambiguous state. Please do the transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP08700001001
Success	Transaction is successful.
DP08700001003
Limit Exceeded	User Exceeds withdrawal transaction limit.
DP08700001004
Invalid Amount	User enters invalid amount.
DP08700001005
Failed	User has dropped in between the transaction.
DP08700001006
In Process	User is in between the process. Transaction in process.
DP08700001007
Insufficient Funds	User has insufficient funds to complete the transaction.
DP08700001008
User Not Allowed	User is not allowed.
DP08700001009
Invalid Initiatee	Initiatee is invalid.
DP08700001010
Not Permitted	User not allowed as payer.
DP08700001011
Do Not Honour	Transaction is already completed.
DP08700001012
Invalid Mobile Number	Invalid mobile number.
DP08700001013
Failed / Refused	The transaction was either failed or refused.
DP08700001014
Transaction Not Allowed	Transaction is not allowed.
DP08700001015
Bad Request	Bad request.
DP08700001016
Transaction Already Exist	This transaction already exists.
DP08700001017
Invalid Paycode	User entered a wrong paycode.
DP08700001018
Paycode Already Used	User entered a paycode which was already used.
DP08700001019
Incorrect MSISDN	User entered a wrong MSISDN.
DP08700001020
Incorrect Currency	User entered wrong currency.
DP08700001021
Incorrect Account / Wallet Details	User entered incorrect account details.
DP08700001022
Incorrect Merchant Code	User entered a wrong merchant code.
DP08700001023Transactions-Summary-APIs
Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Transactions Summary
API will be used to retrieve transactions details of merchant's MSISDN for a defined period.

GET /merchant/v1/transactions

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
x-country	header	string	true	Country of transaction.
x-currency	header	string	true	Currency of transaction.
from	query	integer	true	Date (in EPOCH format) from which transactions are to be fetched.
to	query	integer	true	Date (in EPOCH format) until transactions are to be fetched.
limit	query	integer	true	The number of transactions to be fetched on a page.
offset	query	integer	true	Page number from which transactions are to be fetched.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
Transactions response.
data.count
integer
The number of transactions returned in the response.
data.transactions
object
Reference for the transaction.
transactions.charges
object
Charges.
charges.service
string
Service charge amount.
transactions.payee
object
Payee.
payee.currency
string
The currency in which the payee's wallet was credited.
payee.msisdn
string
MSISDN without the country code of the payee to which the payment was credited.
payee.name
string
Name of the payee to which the payment was credited.
transactions.payer
object
Payer.
payer.currency
string
The currency in which the payer's wallet was debited.
payer.msisdn
string
MSISDN without the country code of the payer from which the payment was debited.
payer.name
string
Name of the payer from which the payment was debited.
transactions.service
object
Service.
service.type
string
A service type of transaction. Possible values are MERCHPAY, CASHIN.
transactions.transaction
object
Transaction
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM.
transaction.amount
string
The amount which was debited from the payer wallet and credited to the payee wallet.
transaction.created_at
number
The timestamp for the transaction initiation.
transaction.id
number
Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction.
transaction.reference_number
string
Reference for the transaction.
transaction.status
string
Transaction status. Possible values TS, TF, and TIP.
status
object
Status.
status.code
number
Status code, HTTP status code.
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action.
status.result_code
string
Application specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.success
boolean
true if no error else false.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.

                    URL obj = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant/v1/transactions");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
  new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
  response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

          RemX
Overview
The RemX suite enables seamless electronic fund transfers across borders, empowering individuals and organizations to send money internationally. It connects various financial institutions and payment service providers, streamlining the remittance process—especially for transactions from non-Airtel ecosystems to Airtel subscribers.

Product Architecture
Flow: Kong Gateway → MFS Platform (API Layer) → MQ (Transaction Processing)

Partners & Use Cases
Currently, both products support transactions from non-Airtel partners (e.g., Thunes, TerraPay) to Airtel subscribers only. Partners can integrate with the Developer Portal APIs to facilitate international remittances to Airtel customers.

Credit: Transfers an amount from a Payer account to a payee account.
Validate: Verifies if a customer is active and able to receive funds.
Refund: Makes a full refund to partners.
Status: Checks the status of the transaction.
Integration & Validation Process
Authorization & Token Generation
Authenticate using your client_id and client_secret to generate an Authorization Token.
API Used: Token Generation (OAuth)
Note: The token is valid for 180 seconds and must be refreshed upon expiry.

KYC (Know Your Customer) Verification
Validate the payee’s KYC details before transferring funds.
API Used: Validate API
Purpose: Ensures the recipient’s wallet is legitimate and active, reducing fraud risk.

Initiating the Transfer
Once the payee is verified and the balance is sufficient, initiate the transfer.
API Used: Credit API
Details Required: Payer and payee wallet information, transfer amount, and related data.

Response Handling
Process the system’s response to the transfer request, which may indicate success, failure, in progress, or an ambiguous state.

Transaction Enquiry
Query the status of a transaction after initiation to check completion or troubleshoot issues.
API Used: Status API

Refunds
Initiate a full refund if required.
API Used: Refund API
Error Handling and Categorization
The Remittance APIs facilitate the electronic transfer of funds across financial institutions and payment service providers, handling international remittances from non-Airtel partners to Airtel subscribers. Throughout the transaction process, robust error handling ensures that both partners and end users are informed of issues promptly and accurately.

Remittance transactions typically involve deducting funds from a partner’s account and crediting the recipient’s Airtel wallet. The process includes multiple steps—such as KYC validation, balance checks, and transfer initiation—each of which may encounter specific error scenarios.

If any issues arise at any stage, the APIs return standardized error codes and messages to indicate the cause of failure. Common error situations include:

Insufficient funds in the partner’s account
Invalid or incomplete KYC details for the recipient
Issues with recipient wallet
Each API response includes an error code and a descriptive message to help partners identify and resolve issues quickly. A list of error codes and their meanings is available in the API documentation, allowing partners to implement precise and user-friendly error handling within their platforms.

By following the prescribed integration steps and handling API responses as documented, partners can ensure seamless and secure remittance experiences for their users.

Flow Diagram

{figure1}

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

User Enquiry API
This Api can be used to do a validation if a customer is active and able to receive funds.

POST /imt/v1/validate

Note: This will only validate that the customer is available and active. It does not validate the amount.
Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
x-country	header	string	true	Transaction Country
x-currency	header	string	true	Transaction Currency
Request Body
Attribute	Description
payee
object
mandatory	payee object
payee.identifier_type
string
mandatory	Type of the identifier
payee.identifier_code
string
mandatory	Value of the identifier
amount
string
optional	Amount to be transacted
Response Body
Attribute	Description
status
object
status object
status.success
boolean
True if no error else false
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action
status.code
string
HTTP status code
status.response_code
string
Application-specific code to identify the error and success response.
data
object
data object
data.first_name
string
Receiver's first name
data.last_name
string
Receiver's last name
data.msisdn
string
Receiver's msisdn
data.business_name
string
Receiver's business name
data.full_name
string
Receiver's fullname
data.user_type
string
Type of the user
data.nickname
string
Receiver's Nickname
data.account_status
string
Receiver's account status: Y(Active), N(Inactive).
data.pin_set
string
True if receiver's AM pin is set else false.
data.user_barred
string
True if receiver is barred else false.
data.bar_type
string
Block type of the user
data.aml_status
string
Receiver's eligibility to receive the given amount.
data.message
string
Message for KYC
data.status
string
Status Code
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.Code samples

                    URL url = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/validate");
HttpURLConnection connection = (HttpURLConnection) url.openConnection();
connection.setRequestMethod("POST");
connection.setRequestProperty("Content-Type", "application/json");
connection.setDoOutput(true);
JSONObject body = new JSONObject();
JSONObject payee = new JSONObject();
payee.put("identifier_type", "nickname");
payee.put("identifier_code", "10352096");
body.put("payee", payee);
body.put("amount", "10");
DataOutputStream wr = new DataOutputStream(connection.getOutputStream());
wr.writeBytes(body.toString());
wr.flush();
wr.close();
int responseCode = connection.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(connection.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

                    <?php
// Define headers array
$headers = array(
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer 10OvEtYZto0QserLGtpekQjeAAadwgaT',
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'x-currency' => 'XAF',
);

// Prepare cURL request
$ch = curl_init();
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_URL, 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/validate');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST, 'POST');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
// Prepare the request body
$request_body = json_decode('{
    "payee":{
        "identifier_type": "nickname",
        "identifier_code": "10352096"
    },
    "amount": "10"
}', true);
$post_data = json_encode($request_body, JSON_UNESCAPED_SLASHES);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, $post_data);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, array(
  'Content-Type: application/json',
  
));
// Execute the cURL request
$response = curl_exec($ch);
if (curl_errno($ch)) {
  echo 'Error:' . curl_error($ch);
}
curl_close($ch);
print_r($response);
?>

           RemX
Overview
The RemX suite enables seamless electronic fund transfers across borders, empowering individuals and organizations to send money internationally. It connects various financial institutions and payment service providers, streamlining the remittance process—especially for transactions from non-Airtel ecosystems to Airtel subscribers.

Product Architecture
Flow: Kong Gateway → MFS Platform (API Layer) → MQ (Transaction Processing)

Partners & Use Cases
Currently, both products support transactions from non-Airtel partners (e.g., Thunes, TerraPay) to Airtel subscribers only. Partners can integrate with the Developer Portal APIs to facilitate international remittances to Airtel customers.

Credit: Transfers an amount from a Payer account to a payee account.
Validate: Verifies if a customer is active and able to receive funds.
Refund: Makes a full refund to partners.
Status: Checks the status of the transaction.
Integration & Validation Process
Authorization & Token Generation
Authenticate using your client_id and client_secret to generate an Authorization Token.
API Used: Token Generation (OAuth)
Note: The token is valid for 180 seconds and must be refreshed upon expiry.

KYC (Know Your Customer) Verification
Validate the payee’s KYC details before transferring funds.
API Used: Validate API
Purpose: Ensures the recipient’s wallet is legitimate and active, reducing fraud risk.

Initiating the Transfer
Once the payee is verified and the balance is sufficient, initiate the transfer.
API Used: Credit API
Details Required: Payer and payee wallet information, transfer amount, and related data.

Response Handling
Process the system’s response to the transfer request, which may indicate success, failure, in progress, or an ambiguous state.

Transaction Enquiry
Query the status of a transaction after initiation to check completion or troubleshoot issues.
API Used: Status API

Refunds
Initiate a full refund if required.
API Used: Refund API
Error Handling and Categorization
The Remittance APIs facilitate the electronic transfer of funds across financial institutions and payment service providers, handling international remittances from non-Airtel partners to Airtel subscribers. Throughout the transaction process, robust error handling ensures that both partners and end users are informed of issues promptly and accurately.

Remittance transactions typically involve deducting funds from a partner’s account and crediting the recipient’s Airtel wallet. The process includes multiple steps—such as KYC validation, balance checks, and transfer initiation—each of which may encounter specific error scenarios.

If any issues arise at any stage, the APIs return standardized error codes and messages to indicate the cause of failure. Common error situations include:

Insufficient funds in the partner’s account
Invalid or incomplete KYC details for the recipient
Issues with recipient wallet
Each API response includes an error code and a descriptive message to help partners identify and resolve issues quickly. A list of error codes and their meanings is available in the API documentation, allowing partners to implement precise and user-friendly error handling within their platforms.

By following the prescribed integration steps and handling API responses as documented, partners can ensure seamless and secure remittance experiences for their users.

Flow Diagram

{figure1}

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Payment API
This API is used to transfer an amount from a Payer account to a payee account.

POST /imt/v1/credit

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
x-country	header	string	true	x-country
x-currency	header	string	true	x-currency
Request Body
Attribute	Description
payee
object
mandatory	payee object
payee.msisdn
string
mandatory	Receiver's msisdn
transaction
object
mandatory	transaction object
transaction.amount
number
mandatory	Amount to be transacted
transaction.id
string
mandatory	Transaction id generated at remittance partner for the transaction. Must be unique for each transaction
transaction.forex_rate
string
mandatory	Forex rate
payer
object
mandatory	payer object
payer.country
string
mandatory	Payer country
payer.first_name
string
mandatory	Payer first name
payer.last_name
string
mandatory	Payer last name
payer.nationality
string
optional	Payer nationality
payer.amount
number
optional	Amount in payer's currency
payer.currency
string
optional	Payer's currency
payer.id_number
string
optional	Payer's id number
payer.address
string
optional	Payer's address
additional_info
object
optional	additional_info object
additional_info.sector_code
string
optional	Sector for the purpose of the transaction
additional_info.purpose_code
string
optional	Purpose of transaction
additional_info.source_of_fund
string
optional	Source of funds
additional_info.transaction_source
string
optional	Source of Transaction
pin
string
mandatory	Encrypted four digit numeric pin to be send in a transaction request to complete the payment
txn_type
string
mandatory	Transaction type
Response Body
Attribute	Description
status
object
status object
status.success
boolean
True if no error else false
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action
status.code
string
HTTP status code
status.response_code
string
Application-specific code to identify the error and success response
data
object
data object
data.transaction
object
transaction object
data.transaction.id
string
Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction
data.transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM
data.transaction.reference_id
string
Reference for the transaction
data.transaction.status
string
Transaction status
data.transaction.message
string
Message
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.Code samples

                   Code samples

                
                    URL url = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/credit");
HttpURLConnection connection = (HttpURLConnection) url.openConnection();
connection.setRequestMethod("POST");
connection.setRequestProperty("Content-Type", "application/json");
connection.setDoOutput(true);
JSONObject body = new JSONObject();
JSONObject payee = new JSONObject();
payee.put("msisdn", "756255985");
body.put("payee", payee);
JSONObject transaction = new JSONObject();
transaction.put("amount", "100");
transaction.put("id", "1747047503");
transaction.put("forex_rate", "0.408171");
body.put("transaction", transaction);
JSONObject payer = new JSONObject();
payer.put("country", "ZM");
payer.put("first_name", "Bob");
payer.put("last_name", "Builder");
payer.put("nationality", "Indian");
payer.put("amount", "200");
payer.put("currency", "ZMW");
payer.put("id_number", "987345");
payer.put("address", "PLOT 197865 Lusaka, Nyumba Yanga");
body.put("payer", payer);
JSONObject additional_info = new JSONObject();
additional_info.put("sector_code", "T9700");
additional_info.put("purpose_code", "10001");
additional_info.put("source_of_fund", "Salary");
additional_info.put("transaction_source", "Bank Transfer");
body.put("additional_info", additional_info);
body.put("pin", "cNydw=");
body.put("txn_type", "MERCHANT_PAYMENT");
DataOutputStream wr = new DataOutputStream(connection.getOutputStream());
wr.writeBytes(body.toString());
wr.flush();
wr.close();
int responseCode = connection.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(connection.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

            PHP Code samples

                
                    <?php
// Define headers array
$headers = array(
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UCc*****ki2w',
  'Accept' => '*/*',
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'x-currency' => 'XAF',
);

// Prepare cURL request
$ch = curl_init();
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_URL, 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/credit');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST, 'POST');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
// Prepare the request body
$request_body = json_decode('{
    "payee": {
        "msisdn": "756255985"
    },
    "transaction": {
        "amount": 100,
        "id": "1747047503",
        "forex_rate": "0.408171"
    },
    "payer": {
        "country": "ZM",
        "first_name": "Bob",
        "last_name": "Builder",
        "nationality": "Indian",
        "amount": 200,
        "currency": "ZMW",
        "id_number": "987345",
        "address": "PLOT 197865 Lusaka, Nyumba Yanga"
    },
    "additional_info": {
        "sector_code": "T9700",
        "purpose_code": "10001",
        "source_of_fund": "Salary",
        "transaction_source": "Bank Transfer"
    },
    "pin": "cNydw=",
    "txn_type": "MERCHANT_PAYMENT"
}', true);
$post_data = json_encode($request_body, JSON_UNESCAPED_SLASHES);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, $post_data);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, array(
  'Content-Type: application/json',
  
));
// Execute the cURL request
$response = curl_exec($ch);
if (curl_errno($ch)) {
  echo 'Error:' . curl_error($ch);
}
curl_close($ch);
print_r($response);
?>

                 RemX
Overview
The RemX suite enables seamless electronic fund transfers across borders, empowering individuals and organizations to send money internationally. It connects various financial institutions and payment service providers, streamlining the remittance process—especially for transactions from non-Airtel ecosystems to Airtel subscribers.

Product Architecture
Flow: Kong Gateway → MFS Platform (API Layer) → MQ (Transaction Processing)

Partners & Use Cases
Currently, both products support transactions from non-Airtel partners (e.g., Thunes, TerraPay) to Airtel subscribers only. Partners can integrate with the Developer Portal APIs to facilitate international remittances to Airtel customers.

Credit: Transfers an amount from a Payer account to a payee account.
Validate: Verifies if a customer is active and able to receive funds.
Refund: Makes a full refund to partners.
Status: Checks the status of the transaction.
Integration & Validation Process
Authorization & Token Generation
Authenticate using your client_id and client_secret to generate an Authorization Token.
API Used: Token Generation (OAuth)
Note: The token is valid for 180 seconds and must be refreshed upon expiry.

KYC (Know Your Customer) Verification
Validate the payee’s KYC details before transferring funds.
API Used: Validate API
Purpose: Ensures the recipient’s wallet is legitimate and active, reducing fraud risk.

Initiating the Transfer
Once the payee is verified and the balance is sufficient, initiate the transfer.
API Used: Credit API
Details Required: Payer and payee wallet information, transfer amount, and related data.

Response Handling
Process the system’s response to the transfer request, which may indicate success, failure, in progress, or an ambiguous state.

Transaction Enquiry
Query the status of a transaction after initiation to check completion or troubleshoot issues.
API Used: Status API

Refunds
Initiate a full refund if required.
API Used: Refund API
Error Handling and Categorization
The Remittance APIs facilitate the electronic transfer of funds across financial institutions and payment service providers, handling international remittances from non-Airtel partners to Airtel subscribers. Throughout the transaction process, robust error handling ensures that both partners and end users are informed of issues promptly and accurately.

Remittance transactions typically involve deducting funds from a partner’s account and crediting the recipient’s Airtel wallet. The process includes multiple steps—such as KYC validation, balance checks, and transfer initiation—each of which may encounter specific error scenarios.

If any issues arise at any stage, the APIs return standardized error codes and messages to indicate the cause of failure. Common error situations include:

Insufficient funds in the partner’s account
Invalid or incomplete KYC details for the recipient
Issues with recipient wallet
Each API response includes an error code and a descriptive message to help partners identify and resolve issues quickly. A list of error codes and their meanings is available in the API documentation, allowing partners to implement precise and user-friendly error handling within their platforms.

By following the prescribed integration steps and handling API responses as documented, partners can ensure seamless and secure remittance experiences for their users.

Flow Diagram

{figure1}

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Check Status API
This API gets the transaction status corresponding to the requested External Id.

GET /imt/v1/checkstatus/{id}

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request
x-country	header	string	true	x-country
x-currency	header	string	true	x-currency
id	path	string	true	Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction
Response Body
Attribute	Description
status
object
status object
status.success
boolean
True if no error else false
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action
status.code
string
HTTP status code
status.response_code
string
Application-specific code to identify the error and success response
data
object
data object
data.transaction
object
transaction object
data.transaction.id
string
Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction
data.transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM
data.transaction.reference_id
string
Reference for the transaction
data.transaction.status
string
Transaction status
data.transaction.message
string
Message
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.Shell                     
                        curl -X GET https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/checkstatus/{id}
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer UCc*****ki2w'
  -H 'Accept: */*'
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'x-country: CG'
  -H 'x-currency: XAF'

                    const headers = {
  'Authorization': 'Bearer UCc*****ki2w',
  'Accept': '*/*',
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'x-country': 'CG',
  'x-currency': 'XAF'
};
fetch('https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/checkstatus/{id}', {
  method: 'GET',
  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
  if (!res.ok) { throw new Error('Request failed with status ' + res.status); }
  return res.json();
})
.then(function(body) {
  console.log(body);
})
.catch(function(error) {
  console.error('Error:', error);
});

Refund API
This API is used to make full refunds to Partners.

POST /imt/v1/refund

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request
x-country	header	string	true	x-country
x-currency	header	string	true	x-currency
Request Body
Attribute	Description
transaction
object
mandatory	transaction object
transaction.id
string
mandatory	Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction
pin
string
mandatory	Encrypted four digit numeric pin to be send in a transaction request to complete the payment
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
data object
data.transaction
object
transaction object
data.transaction.id
string
Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction
data.transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM
data.transaction.message
string
Message
reference_id
string
Reference for the transaction
data.transaction.status
string
Transaction status
status
object
status object
status.code
string
HTTP status code
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action
status.response_code
string
Application-specific code to identify the error and success response
status.success
boolean
True if no error else false
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.

};
const headers = {
  'Authorization': 'Bearer UCc*****ki2w',
  'Accept': '*/*',
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'x-country': 'CG',
  'x-currency': 'XAF'
};
fetch('https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/refund', {
  method: 'POST',
  body: inputBody,
  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
  if (!res.ok) { throw new Error('Request failed with status ' + res.status); }
  return res.json();
})
.then(function(body) {
  console.log(body);
})
.catch(function(error) {
  console.error('Error:', error);
});

                
                
Body parameter

                        
                            {
    "transaction": {
        "id": "1753167656"
    },
    "pin": "d***lQ="
}

            "id": "1754303725",

"reference_id": "APCCG173533944374656",
            "message": "Transaction Reversed",
            "status": "TR"
        }
    },
    "status": {
        "code": "200",
        "message": "Success",
        "response_code": "DP03520001001",
        "success": true
    }
}
                        
         Java Code samples

                
                    URL url = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/refund");
HttpURLConnection connection = (HttpURLConnection) url.openConnection();
connection.setRequestMethod("POST");
connection.setRequestProperty("Content-Type", "application/json");
connection.setDoOutput(true);
JSONObject body = new JSONObject();
JSONObject transaction = new JSONObject();
transaction.put("id", "1753167656");
body.put("transaction", transaction);
body.put("pin", "d***lQ=");
DataOutputStream wr = new DataOutputStream(connection.getOutputStream());
wr.writeBytes(body.toString());
wr.flush();
wr.close();
int responseCode = connection.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(connection.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

            PHP                
                    <?php
// Define headers array
$headers = array(
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UCc*****ki2w',
  'Accept' => '*/*',
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'x-currency' => 'XAF',
);

// Prepare cURL request
$ch = curl_init();
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_URL, 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/refund');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST, 'POST');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
// Prepare the request body
$request_body = json_decode('{
    "transaction": {
        "id": "1753167656"
    },
    "pin": "d***lQ="
}', true);
$post_data = json_encode($request_body, JSON_UNESCAPED_SLASHES);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, $post_data);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, array(
  'Content-Type: application/json',
  
));
// Execute the cURL request
$response = curl_exec($ch);
if (curl_errno($ch)) {
  echo 'Error:' . curl_error($ch);
}
curl_close($ch);
print_r($response);
?>

           RemX
Overview
The RemX suite enables seamless electronic fund transfers across borders, empowering individuals and organizations to send money internationally. It connects various financial institutions and payment service providers, streamlining the remittance process—especially for transactions from non-Airtel ecosystems to Airtel subscribers.

Product Architecture
Flow: Kong Gateway → MFS Platform (API Layer) → MQ (Transaction Processing)

Partners & Use Cases
Currently, both products support transactions from non-Airtel partners (e.g., Thunes, TerraPay) to Airtel subscribers only. Partners can integrate with the Developer Portal APIs to facilitate international remittances to Airtel customers.

Credit: Transfers an amount from a Payer account to a payee account.
Validate: Verifies if a customer is active and able to receive funds.
Refund: Makes a full refund to partners.
Status: Checks the status of the transaction.
Integration & Validation Process
Authorization & Token Generation
Authenticate using your client_id and client_secret to generate an Authorization Token.
API Used: Token Generation (OAuth)
Note: The token is valid for 180 seconds and must be refreshed upon expiry.

KYC (Know Your Customer) Verification
Validate the payee’s KYC details before transferring funds.
API Used: Validate API
Purpose: Ensures the recipient’s wallet is legitimate and active, reducing fraud risk.

Initiating the Transfer
Once the payee is verified and the balance is sufficient, initiate the transfer.
API Used: Credit API
Details Required: Payer and payee wallet information, transfer amount, and related data.

Response Handling
Process the system’s response to the transfer request, which may indicate success, failure, in progress, or an ambiguous state.

Transaction Enquiry
Query the status of a transaction after initiation to check completion or troubleshoot issues.
API Used: Status API

Refunds
Initiate a full refund if required.
API Used: Refund API
Error Handling and Categorization
The Remittance APIs facilitate the electronic transfer of funds across financial institutions and payment service providers, handling international remittances from non-Airtel partners to Airtel subscribers. Throughout the transaction process, robust error handling ensures that both partners and end users are informed of issues promptly and accurately.

Remittance transactions typically involve deducting funds from a partner’s account and crediting the recipient’s Airtel wallet. The process includes multiple steps—such as KYC validation, balance checks, and transfer initiation—each of which may encounter specific error scenarios.

If any issues arise at any stage, the APIs return standardized error codes and messages to indicate the cause of failure. Common error situations include:

Insufficient funds in the partner’s account
Invalid or incomplete KYC details for the recipient
Issues with recipient wallet
Each API response includes an error code and a descriptive message to help partners identify and resolve issues quickly. A list of error codes and their meanings is available in the API documentation, allowing partners to implement precise and user-friendly error handling within their platforms.

By following the prescribed integration steps and handling API responses as documented, partners can ensure seamless and secure remittance experiences for their users.

Flow Diagram

{figure1}

Error Codes
Error Code	Expected Error Reason	Description
DP03570001000
Ambiguous	The transaction is still processing and is in ambiguous state. Please do the transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP03570001001
Success	Transaction is successful.
DP03570001002
User Enquiry Failed	User enquiry failed or user not found.
DP03570001003
Bad Request	Bad request or invalid input.
DP03570001004
Invalid Amount	User enters invalid amount.
DP03570001005
User has dropped in between the transaction	User has dropped in between the transaction
DP03570001006
Transaction In Progress	User is in between the process. Transaction in process.
DP03570001007
Insufficient Funds	User has insufficient funds to complete the transaction.
DP03570001009
Withdrawal transaction limit exceeded	Withdrawal transaction limit exceeded.
DP03570001010
Something Went Wrong	Unknown or unclassified error or technical error.
DP03570001011
Maximum Transaction Limit Reached	Maximum transaction Limit reached.
DP03570001012
Transaction Failed	Transaction Failed
DP03570001013
Invalid Payer or Payee	Invalid Payer or Payee
DP03570001014
Invalid mobile number	Invalid mobile number
DP03570001015
Transaction Not Found	Transaction Not Found
DP03570001016
Duplicate transaction ID	Duplicate transaction ID
DP03570001017
MSISDN Barred / MSISDN locked	MSISDN Barred / MSISDN locked
DP03570001018
Transaction Timed out	Transaction Timed out
DP03570001019
Invalid Amount	Amount entered is less than the minimum allowed value
DP03570001020
Maximum amount allowed for the day limit reached	Maximum amount allowed for the day limit reached Billers Callback
Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Billers Callback
Partner will update the status of async customer initiated transaction, based on which transaction will be marked as SUCCESS or FAILURE.

POST /arch-in/web/callback/billers/{partnerCode}

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
partnerCode	path	string	true	Partner code
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
x-country	header	string	true	Transaction country.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
Request Body
Attribute	Description
txnRes
object
mandatory	Transaction response.
txnRes.authCode
string
mandatory	Partner transaction id.
txnRes.id
string
mandatory	Airtel money transaction id.
txnRes.partnerResponse
object
optional	Object for sending additional values.
txnRes.processingStatus
string
mandatory	Transaction Status. Example: PROCESSED, REJECTED.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
msg
string
Message description.
msgid
string
Message id.
st
boolean
Status.
timestamp
string
Response timestamp.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication. Shell                    
                        curl -X POST https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/arch-in/web/callback/billers/{partnerCode}
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'Accept: application/json'
  -H 'x-country: CG'
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer  UC****2w'

  'Authorization' => 'Bearer  UC****2w'
);

// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('POST', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/arch-in/web/callback/billers/{partnerCode}', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

KYC
This API is used to fetch KYC Information of Users based on alias or msisdn.

POST /arch-in/web/callback/loans/user-kyc/{partnerCode}

This version will be deprecated soon. Please migrate to version 2.0.
Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
partnerCode	path	string	true	Partner code
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
x-country	header	string	true	Country Code
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
Request Body
Attribute	Description
alias
string
optional	Hashed msisdn of User for which KYC is requested. Either msisdn or alias field should be present.
msisdn
string
optional	Msisdn of User for which KYC is requested. Either msisdn or alias field should be present.
partnerCode
string
optional	The partner code, who is requesting for KYC
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
Response data.
data.entityName
string
Represents the Business name of the merchant/agent or full name of individual in case of subscribers.
data.firstName
string
Represents the first name of an individual (agent/subscriber). Can be ignored for merchants.
data.hashedMsisdn
string
Hashed msisdn of User for which KYC is requested.
data.lastName
string
Represents the last name of an individual (agent/subscriber). Can be ignored for merchants.
data.msisdn
string
Customer's MSISDN as is.
msg
string
Status message
msgid
string
Response code
st
boolean
Status Flag
timestamp
string
Current Timestamp
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.Shell

  -H 'Authorization: Bearer UCc*****ki2w'

                    Bank to Wallet
Content Type
JSON
Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Payment
This API facilitates banks to transfer funds from a bank account to airtel user's AM wallet

POST /bank-to-wallet/v1/payments/

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	The "Accept" header is an HTTP header that specifies the media types that the client can understand or process
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
x-country	header	string	true	Transaction country.
x-currency	header	string	true	Transaction currency.
Request Body
Attribute	Description
transaction
object
mandatory	Information about the bank to wallet transaction.
transaction.id
string
mandatory	A unique identifier for the transaction.
transaction.amount
string
mandatory	The amount of money involved in the transaction.
transaction.payee
object
mandatory	Information about the recipient of the transaction.
payee.address_type
string
mandatory	Specifies the type of address used for the payee. Supported value is MOBILE
payee.msisdn
string
mandatory	MSISDN of the payee.
note
string
mandatory	Additional remarks or comments related to the transaction.
additional_info
object
optional	This field is reserved for future use. It allows for passing any additional fields that may be introduced in future versions of the system without altering the existing structure. Any extra details not covered by the predefined parameters can be included here.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
Contains the data related to the transaction.
data.additional_info
object
extra details not covered by the predefined parameters can be included here.
additional_info.mq_txn_id
string
Mobiquity Transaction Id
data.transaction
object
Information about the transaction.
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
The ID associated with the transaction in Airtel Money system.
transaction.reference_id
string
A reference ID associated with the transaction.
transaction.id
string
The unique identifier for the transaction sent in the request.
transaction.status
string
Status of the Transaction
transaction.message
string
A message providing information about the status of the transaction.
status
object
Contains the status of the response.
status.response_code
string
Product specific codes are used to identify both error and success responses, and these codes will vary depending on the type of error or success encountered.
status.code
string
An HTTP status code.
status.success
boolean
true if no error else false.
status.message
string
A message providing information about the status of the response.
This operation requires Oauth2 authentication. Shell Code samples

                    URL obj = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/bank-to-wallet/v1/payments/");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("POST");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
  new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
  response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

               PHP                
                    <?php
require 'vendor/autoload.php';
$headers = array(
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'Accept' => 'application/json',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UCLcp1oeq44KPXr8X*******xCzki2w',
  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'x-currency' => 'XAF'
);
$client = new GuzzleHttpClient();
// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('POST', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/bank-to-wallet/v1/payments/', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

            Bank to Wallet
Content Type
JSON
Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Transaction Enquiry
This api can be used to fetch transaction status

GET /bank-to-wallet/v1/payments/{id}

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
id	path	string	true	A unique identifier for the transaction.
Accept	header	string	true	The "Accept" header is an HTTP header that specifies the media types that the client can understand or process
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
x-country	header	string	true	Transaction country.
x-currency	header	string	true	Transaction currency.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
Contains the data related to the transaction.
data.additional_info
object
extra details not covered by the predefined parameters can be included here.
additional_info.mq_txn_id
string
Mobiquity transaction Id
data.transaction
object
Information about the transaction.
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
The ID associated with the transaction in Airtel Money system.
transaction.reference_id
string
A reference ID associated with the transaction.
transaction.id
string
The unique identifier for the transaction sent in the request.
transaction.status
string
Status of the Transaction
transaction.message
string
A message providing information about the status of the transaction.
status
object
Contains the status of the response.
status.response_code
string
Product specific codes are used to identify both error and success responses, and these codes will vary depending on the type of error or success encountered.
status.code
string
An HTTP status code.
status.success
boolean
true if no error else false.
status.message
string
A message providing information about the status of the response.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.Code samples

               Bank to Wallet
Content Type
JSON
Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Bank to Wallet API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team
Error Code	Expected Error Reason	Description
DP02100001000
Ambiguous	The transaction is still processing or is in ambiguous state. Please perform transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP02100001001
Success	Transaction is successful.
DP02100001002
Duplicate Transaction ID	Duplicate Transaction ID. Please use a unique transaction id.
DP02100001003
Transaction Time Out	Transaction Timed Out. Please perform transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP02100001004
Transaction Not Found	Transaction is not found . Please verify the transaction id and enquire again.
DP02100001005
Failed	Transaction failed or refused.
DP02100001006
Transaction In Progress	The transaction is still in progress. Please perform a transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP02100001007
Invalid Input	One of the input entered is invalid.MERCHANT COLLECTION
Merchant collection requires the Merchant to register their micro-merchants on airtel through dev portal . They can now use merchant collection product to pull funds from airtel subscribers to their micro-merchant wallet.

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Register Merchant
This API is to register a submerchant of a particular merchant

POST /merchant-collection/v1/merchant

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
x-country	header	string	true	x-country
x-currency	header	string	true	x-currency
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization
Content-Type	header	string	true	Content-Type
Accept	header	string	true	Accept
Request Body
Attribute	Description
merchants
array
mandatory	merchants
merchants.relationship_id
string
mandatory	merchants.relationship_id
merchants.name
string
mandatory	merchants.name
merchants[0].account_details
object
mandatory	merchants[0].account_details
merchants.account_details.msisdn
string
mandatory	merchants.account_details.msisdn
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object

data.merchants
array
data.merchants

string
data.merchants.relationship_id
data.merchants.name
string
data.merchants.name

string
data.merchants.airtel_relationship_id

  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'Accept': 'application/json'
};
fetch('https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant-collection/v1/merchant',{
  method: 'POST',
  body: inputBody,
  headers: headers
}).then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

      }
    }
  ]

               Java                
                    URL obj = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant-collection/v1/merchant");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("POST");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
  new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
  response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

             PHP Code samples

                
                    <?php
require 'vendor/autoload.php';
$headers = array(
  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'x-currency' => 'XAF',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer j3cvwSYMvH5prycoDY8Wm0Zu39iip1UB',
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'Accept' => 'application/json'
);
$client = new GuzzleHttpClient();
// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('POST', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant-collection/v1/merchant', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

             MERCHANT COLLECTION
Merchant collection requires the Merchant to register their micro-merchants on airtel through dev portal . They can now use merchant collection product to pull funds from airtel subscribers to their micro-merchant wallet.

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Fetch registered merchant
This API is used to fetch the registered micro merchant

GET /merchant-collection/v1/fetch

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
x-country	header	string	true	x-country
x-currency	header	string	true	x-currency
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization
Content-Type	header	string	true	Content-Type
Accept	header	string	true	Accept
relationship_id	query	string	true	Aggregator assigned unique id for its merchant
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
data
data.merchants
array
data.merchants
data.merchants.relationship_id
string
data.merchants.relationship_id
data.merchants.name
string
data.merchants.name
data.merchants.airtel_relationship_id
string
data.merchants.airtel_relationship_id
data.merchants[0].account_details
object
data.merchants[0].account_details
data.merchants.account_details.msisdn
string
data.merchants.account_details.msisdn
data.merchants.status
string
data.merchants.status
data.merchants[0].additional_info
object
data.merchants[0].additional_info
data.merchants.created_at
string
data.merchants.created_at
data.merchants.updated_at
string
data.merchants.updated_at
status
object
status
status.success
boolean
status.success
status.message
string
status.message
status.code
string
status.code
status.response_code
string
status.response_code
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication. Shell

  -H 'x-currency: XAF'

  -H 'Accept: application/json'

        MERCHANT COLLECTION
Merchant collection requires the Merchant to register their micro-merchants on airtel through dev portal . They can now use merchant collection product to pull funds from airtel subscribers to their micro-merchant wallet.

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Transfers
This API is used to initiate the transaction

POST /merchant-collection/v1/payments

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
x-country	header	string	true	x-country
x-currency	header	string	true	x-currency
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization
Content-Type	header	string	true	Content-Type
Accept	header	string	true	Accept
Request Body
Attribute	Description
payee
object
mandatory	Payee.
payee.relationship_id
string
mandatory	Aggregator assigned unique id for its merchant. Recommended to use 6 length.
payer
object
mandatory	Payer.
payer.msisdn
string
mandatory	Payer msisdn.
transaction
object
mandatory	Transaction object.
transaction.id
string
mandatory	Unique Id for transaction.
transaction.amount
string
mandatory	Amount to be transffered.
transaction.reference
string
mandatory	Reference for the transaction to identify payment in transaction reports. max allowed length is 25.
additional_info
object
optional	Additional info regarding transactions can be passed in this json block
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
data
data.transaction
object
data.transaction
data.transaction.id
string
data.transaction.id
data.transaction.airtel_money_id
string
data.transaction.airtel_money_id
data.transaction.status
string
data.transaction.status
data.transaction.message
string
data.transaction.message
data.additional_info
object
data.additional_info
status
object
status
status.success
boolean
status.success
status.message
string
status.message
status.code
string
status.code
status.response_code
string
status.response_code
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication. Shell                    
                        curl -X POST https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant-collection/v1/payments
  -H 'x-country: CG'
  -H 'x-currency: XAF'
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer j3cvwSYMvH5prycoDY8Wm0Zu39iip1UB'
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'Accept: application/json'

    "payee": {

        "msisdn": "123****89"

        "id": "TEST1",
        "amount": "1000",
        "reference": "Fine Works Test Transaction"
    },
    "additional_info": {}
}

            "status": "TIP",
            "message": "Transaction is in progress."
        },
        "additional_info": {
        }
 
    },
    "status": {
        "success": true,
        "message": "Success",
        "code": "200",
        "response_code": "DP03520001001"
    }

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Payment enquiry
This API is used for payment enquiry

GET /merchant-collection/v1/payments/{id}

                    const headers = {
  'Accept': '*/* ',
  'X-Country': 'CG',
  'X-Currency': 'XAF',
  'Authorization': 'Bearer UC*******2w'
};
fetch('https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant-collection/v1/payments/{id}',{
  method: 'GET',
  headers: headers
}).then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

} Java                
                    URL obj = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant-collection/v1/payments/{id}");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
  new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
  response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

                    PHP                
                    <?php
require 'vendor/autoload.php';
$headers = array(
  'Accept' => '*/* ',
  'X-Country' => 'CG',
  'X-Currency' => 'XAF',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UC*******2w'
);
$client = new GuzzleHttpClient();
// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('GET', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant-collection/v1/payments/{id}', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}
                
                
200 responses

                        
                            {

           MERCHANT COLLECTION
Merchant collection requires the Merchant to register their micro-merchants on airtel through dev portal . They can now use merchant collection product to pull funds from airtel subscribers to their micro-merchant wallet.

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Refund Payment
This API is used to initiate refund process for any complete transaction

POST /merchant-collection/v1/payments/refund

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Accept	header	string	true	Accept
Content-Type	header	string	true	Content-Type
X-Country	header	string	true	X-Country
X-Currency	header	string	true	X-Currency
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization
Request Body
Attribute	Description
transaction
object
mandatory	transaction
transaction.id
string
mandatory	transaction.id

Attribute	Description

                        curl -X POST https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant-collection/v1/payments/refund
  -H 'Accept: */* '
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'X-Country: CG'
  -H 'X-Currency: XAF'
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer UC*******2w'


                    
                
Body parameter

                        
                            {
    "transaction": {
        "id": "TEST1"
    }
}
                        
                    
200 responses

                        
                            
{
    "data": {
        "transaction": {
            "id": "TEST1",
            "airtel_money_id": "ACOLL-ABCDEFGH-PQRSTU-TEST1",
            "status": "TR",
            "message": "Transaction Reversed."
        },
        "additional_info": {
        }
 
    },
    "status": {
        "success": true,
        "message": "Success",
        "code": "200",
        "response_code": "DP03520001001"
    }
}
