                   Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Platforms API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team

Common Error Codes
Code	Description	
ROUTER001	Wallet is not configured -- Your Application Wallet is not configured. So we cannot process your request.	
ROUTER003	Missing header/body params -- The request failed because mandatory request header or body is missing.	
ROUTER005	Country route not configured -- The request failed due to country route is not configured. Please contact customer support.	
ROUTER006	Invalid Country -- The request failed due to incorrect value of country code in the request	
ROUTER007	User Not authorized -- You are not authorized to perform any operations in the provided country. So we cannot process your request.	
ROUTER112	Invalid Currency -- The request failed due to incorrect currency code in the request	
ROUTER114	Error while Validating Pin -- The request failed due to error occured while validating Pin in the request.	
ROUTER115	Incorrect Pin -- The request failed due to incorrect pin in the request	
ROUTER116	Incorrect Encrypted Pin -- The encrypted value of the PIN is incorrect. Please review the encryption mechanism. The request has failed.	
ROUTER117	Request Timeout -- The request has timed out. In case of payments/refund, please perform a transaction enquiry; otherwise, try again later.	
ROUTER119	Invalid/Missing Currency -- The request failed due to an invalid or missing currency in the request parameters.Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Platforms API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team

Product Specific Error Codes
Code	Description	
ESB000001	Something went wrong. The request can be in ambiguous state please do a transaction enquiry.	
ESB000004	An error occurred while initiating the payment. The request can be in ambiguous state please do a transaction enquiry.	
ESB000008	Field validation Error. The request can be in ambiguous state please do a transaction enquiry.	
ESB000010	Request was Success.	
ESB000011	Request was Failed.	
ESB000014	An error occurred while fetching the transaction status. The request can be in ambiguous state please do a transaction enquiry after some time.	
ESB000033	The request has been failed due to Invalid MSISDN Length. Please try again with correct request.	
ESB000034	The request has been failed due to Invalid Country Name. Please try again with correct request.	
ESB000035	The request has been failed due to Invalid Currency Code. Please try again with correct request.	
ESB000036	The request has been failed due to either it is Invalid MSISDN Length or it doesn't starts with 0. Please try again with correct request.	
ESB000039	Vendor is not configured to do transaction in the provided country. Please try again with correct request.	
ESB000041	Transaction with External Transaction ID already exists. Please try again with correct request.	
ESB000045	No Transaction Found With Provided Transaction Id.	
0000900	Transaction might be in ambigous state. Please check response_code to know the exact status or perform a transaction enquiry.Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Platforms API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team

HTTP Error Codes
Code	Description	
400	Bad Request -- Your request is invalid.	
401	Unauthorized -- Your API key or bearer token is incorrect.	
403	Forbidden -- The requested item is hidden for administrators only.	
404	Not Found -- The specified path could not be found.	
405	Method Not Allowed -- You tried to access a path with an invalid method.	
408	Read Timeout -- The request has timed out. In case of payments/refund, please perform an transaction enquiry; otherwise, try again later.	
429	Too Many Requests -- You're requesting too many requests! Slow down.	
500	Internal Server Error -- We had a problem with our server. Try again later.	
503	Service Unavailable -- We're temporarily offline for maintenance. Please try again later.	
502	Bad gateway -- In case of payments/refund, please perform an transaction enquiry; otherwise, try again later.	
504	Gateway Timeout -- In case of payments/refund, please perform an transaction enquiry; otherwise, try again later.Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Platforms API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team

Response Types
Code	Description	
200	Request Accepted.	
401	Unauthorized Request.	
403	Forbidden Request.	
404	Resource Not Found.	References
Our API supports merchants in a number of countries and supports currencies depending on the payment type, Remittance, Collection, etc.

Banking


BANK TO WALLET

Est un service qui permet aux abonnés Airtel Money de faire des transferts d’argent d’un compte bancaire vers un compte Airtel Money et inversement. Faites des transactions depuis chez vous avec nos banques partenaires.

Composer *128*4# et suivre les instructions

Choisir votre Banque,

Choisir le type d’action à mener :

*Tapez 1 : pour le virement Airtel Money vers la banque, transférez de l’argent de votre compte Airtel Money vers votre Banque

*Tapez 2 : pour le virement de la banque vers Airtel Money, transférez de l’argent de votre compte bancaire vers votre compte Airtel Money

· Saisissez le montant à transférer

· Saisissez le mot de passe ou code Pin.

*Tapez 3 : pour consultez votre solde bancaire

*Tapez 4 : pour obtenir un mini relevé

Nos banques partenaires : UBA BANK - LCB Bank – ECOBANK – BSCA – BGFIBank - BCI – MUCODEC – BCH - CREDIT DU CONGO.

Type d’opération	Coût
Banque vers Airtel	0 F CFA
Airtel Money Vers la Banque	1%
Consultation Solde	0 F CFA
Consultation Mini relevé	0 F CFA
Retrait d’argent GAB MUCODEC

Grace au partenariat entre Airtel Money et la Mucodec, retirer 24h/24 et 7j/7 de gros montant, uniquement des multiples de 5000 (5.000 ; 10.000 ;15.000 etc.) ou que vous soyez dans tous les GAB de la Mucodec.

· Etape 1 : composez *128#

· Etape 2 : choisir option 6

· Etape 3 : choisir option 2

· Etape 4 : saisissez le montant – le montant de la transaction doit être un multiple de 5000 (5.000 ; 10.000 ; 15.000 ; 20.000 etc.)

· Etape 5 : vous recevrez un sms avec le code de retrait valide 15 min

· Etape 6 : rendez-vous devant le guichet Mucodec,

o Cliquez sur l’option « service sans carte »

o Ensuite sur « Airtel Money »

o Saisissez votre numéro de téléphone

o Saisissez le code reçu par sms et

o Saisissez le montant et validez

Appels locaux
Niger
Appels locaux
Grâce à ses offres prépayées, Airtel vous permet de communiquer librement et aux meilleurs tarifs du marché. Hors forfait, vos appels sont facturés par seconde vers tous les réseaux locaux à :

· 1,85 FCFA par seconde vers Airtel
· 2,17 FCFA par seconde vers les autres réseaux
Vous aurez sûrement besoin de plus d’informations complémentaires pour mieux vous orienter dans votre choix. Vous pouvez nous contacter via notre adresse email service clients : Serviceclients@cg.airtel.com

Notre centre d’appels : le 121, est à votre entière disposition tous les jours 24/24.

Le tarif d'appel est de 24 FCFA/appel.

appel international

appel international
Appelez vos proches à l'international à des prix exceptionnels et gardez le contact avec eux durant vos voyages avec les forfaits roaming
1	Select International
Calling and Roaming


Appel à l'international

appel international
Avec airtel, l'appel international est automatiquement disponible sur tous les téléphones.

comment appeler à l'international? 

Composer simplement "+" ou "00" (code d'accès à l'international) suivi chronologiquement par les codes suivants:

Indicatif du pays de destination

Code de zone (en cas de besoin)

Numéro de téléphone de votre correspondant

Profiter des meilleurs Tarifs vers l’international avec le Réseau des Smartphones.

NB: tous les appels sont factures à la seconde.*

SMS Bundles

Airtel to Airtel Bundles

One subscribes to a bundle & can send SMS for as low as 2.5/- to Airtel . This is a huge saving as a standard onnet SMS costs 100/=.

Sms Bundles	Direct Activation	What I Get	Validity	Price
100 SMS	*180*1*1#	100 sms	24 hours	250/=
Daily PLUS (320) sms	*180*1*2#	320 sms	24 hours	500/=
700SMS (weekly)	*180*1*3#	700 sms	1 week	1,000/=
4000 sms	*180*1*4#	4000 sms	1 month	5,000/=
 

Across All Networks

One subscribes to a bundle & can send SMS for as low as 40/= to Airtel customers & across networks that is Airtel, Orange, Smart, K2, UTL & MTN. This is a huge saving as a standard  offnet SMS costs 130/=

Sms Bundles	Direct Activation	What I Get	Validity	Price
10 SMS	*180*2*1#	10 sms	30 Days	500/=
25 SMS	*180*2*2#	25 sms	30 Days	1,000/=
50 SMS	*180*2*3#	50 sms	30 Days	2,000/=
125 SMS	*180*2*4#	125 sms	30 Days	5,000/=
SMS Gifting

You can also gift your friends an SMS pack. Press *180#, Select option 3.

NB: This is an Auto renewal service. To Cancel Auto renewal, press *180#, Select option 4.

Forfaits Roaming OneAirtel


Forfaits Roaming OneAirtel
Profitez de nos tarifs spéciaux lors de vos déplacements dans les pays Airtel.

Le One Airtel c’est le Roaming dans les pays Airtel. Grâce à ce service, les abonnés airtel (Postpayés/Prepayés) peuvent utiliser les services airtel (Appels/SMS/internet) dans tous les 14 pays (Gabon, Kenya, Madagascar, Malawi, Niger, Nigeria, RDC, Rwanda, Seychelles, Tanzanie, Tchad, Ouganda, Zambie) ou Airtel est présent à des tarifs réduits.

Free Roaming
banner-image
Free Roaming
L’offre « Free Roaming » vous donne la possibilité de communiquer à l’étranger au tarif local du pays d’origine (Congo Brazzaville).

1	Choisissez votre pays de destination
Gabon
SERVICES	TARIFS
Appel Local	158 frs/min
Appel vers le Congo	158 frs/min
Appel à l'international	689,5 frs/min
Cout de la réception d'appel après 300 minutes	303,6 frs/min
SMS/texto	50 frs/SMS
DATA/10kb après 500 MG	0,7 Frs/10KB
Comment se fait l’activation?
L’activation est automatique.

Quels sont les avantages?
Les réceptions d’appels et SMS sont gratuites.

La communication se fait au tarif du Congo dans le pays visité ou de destination

Des sons illimités pour
faire plaisir à vos amis


Service Offert	Description	Numéros ou Commande
SOS Crédit	Service qui permet d'emprunter du crédit, remboursable dès la prochaine recharge	Pour y accéder, composez *143# ou *121*5# OK/YES
Ziki Na Biso	C'est un service qui permet aux abonnés Airtel de télécharger des sons pour en faire des tonalités d'attente que leurs correspondant pourront écouter au moment de l'appel	Appelez le 188 ou envoyez par sms le mot-clé "SUB" au 188
CTQUI	C'est un service qui permet de recevoir une alerte SMS signalant un appel manqué	Appelez le 121 pour toute activation
Rappelle-Moi	Service qui permet à un abonné de demander à un autre abonné de le rappeler	Compposez *1050#numéro du correspondant. Ex: *1050*050000000#
Connaître son numéro	Service qui permet de connaître son numéro de téléphone	Composez le *120# ou *121*2#
Me2U ou CpourTOI	Service de transfert de crédit, qui permet d'envoyer depuis son compte, du crédit au correspondant airtel de son choix	Composez *121*5*2#

Taxi Connect

COMMENT CA MARCHE?

1. Montez dans un taxi équipé Airtel Monnaie;

2. Pour payez votre course, composez *128*1000# et suivez les instructions ;

3. Validez le paiement et recevez votre confirmation instantanément.

 

GAGNEZ DES BONUS A CHAQUE COURSE!

Avec Airtel money, chaque course peut vous faire gagner des minutes d’appels, des forfaits internet, des bons d’essence …

Avec Taxi Connect, payez votre course en un clin d’œil!

English
CONGOB
Getting Started
Start Building with Airtel Africa
Airtel Africa provides a secure way to accept money from the customers.
Create an account or login using your existing credentials. If you don't have an account then do signup.
Generate your staging account credentials from the application settings. These are required to explore Airtel Africa's integration solutions.
When you are ready to go live, activate your account in the dashboard to get production account credentials.

Understanding account credentials
Account credentials are available in your dashboard for both staging and production environment.
Remember: Never share your application key or secret with other developers or anyone at Airtel Africa as this will compromise the security of your application.

Documentation
Read the instructions about how to effectively use and integrate with an API

ShellRubyPythonJavascriptJavaPHP
Authorization
Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

OAuth2
This API is used to get the bearer token. The output of this API contains access_token that will be used as bearer token for the API that we will be going to call.

POST /auth/oauth2/token

Note : This is a common API to fetch access token for all the APIs listed in the documentation section.
Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Content-Type	header	string	true	specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	specifies the media type of the response.
Request Body
Attribute	Description
client_id
string
mandatory	The client_id is a public identifier for apps. It must also be unique across all clients that the authorization server handles. This is equivalent to consumer key displayed under keys section of application listing. Example: c02e9e46-db9d-4faf-b91a-94f88bbe688c
client_secret
string
mandatory	The client_secret is a secret known only to the application and the authorization server.This is equivalent to consumer secret displayed under keys section of application listing Example: ab672211-4197-4c11-ba79-b29ce2034ca2
grant_type
string
mandatory	The Client Credential grant type is used by confidential and public clients to fetch access token. Example: client_credentials
Response Body
Attribute	Description
access_token
string
Received token of the user.
expires_in
string
Expiry time of the access-token received.
token_type
string
Type of token.
This operation is required to fetch OAuth2 Access token.Shell Code samples

                       Java                
                    URL obj = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/auth/oauth2/token");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("POST");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
  new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
  response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

                    <?php
require 'vendor/autoload.php';
$headers = array(
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'Accept' => '*/*'
);
$client = new GuzzleHttpClient();
// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('POST', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/auth/oauth2/token', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

                        Encryption
Version
2.0
Always stay ahead with the latest version! Older versions may be deprecated at any time, so for the best experience and support, we recommend using the most up-to-date API. Keep your integration future-proof!

Version Highlights:

As part of our security enhancements, we now support RSA encryption with 2048-bit keys and a new algorithm for PIN encryption. We highly recommend transitioning to this stronger security mechanism for improved protection. All other interactions will remain unchanged.

Encryption documentation details methods for encrypting sensitive data, including PINs and callbacks, ensuring secure handling and protection against unauthorized access.

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Pin Encryption
An RSA user creates and publishes a public key based on two large prime numbers, along with an auxiliary value. The prime numbers are kept secret. Messages can be encrypted by anyone, via the published public key, but can only be decoded by private key.

RSA encryption strategy with mode ECB, padding OAEPWithSHA-256AndMGF1Padding and KeyLength 2048 is used to encrypt data at client side..

It can be done using the below given code snippet for RSA encryption.


Note : Public key is available in code snippet. Copy it from there.Public Key

                    
                           Encryption
Version
2.0
Always stay ahead with the latest version! Older versions may be deprecated at any time, so for the best experience and support, we recommend using the most up-to-date API. Keep your integration future-proof!

Version Highlights:

As part of our security enhancements, we now support RSA encryption with 2048-bit keys and a new algorithm for PIN encryption. We highly recommend transitioning to this stronger security mechanism for improved protection. All other interactions will remain unchanged.

Encryption documentation details methods for encrypting sensitive data, including PINs and callbacks, ensuring secure handling and protection against unauthorized access.

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Callback Encryption
Partner Can Authenticate the response sent to them regarding the status of the transaction on the callback url. It can be configured in Application settings. Along with the callback request body a , hash message will be sent to user. User can hash the the callback request with the private key shown in application setting using HmacSHA256 algorithm, with output text format in Base64 and can match with it hash message sent if Callback authentication is enabled.

SHA256 with RSA signature is an efficient asymmetric encryption method used in many secure APIs. This algorithm first calculates a unique hash of the input data using SHA256 algorithm. The hash is then encrypted with a private key using the RSA algorithm. Steps to enable callback authentication

Step1 : Go to application settings

Step 2: Enable callback authentication

Step 3: Copy Private key to encrypt using HMAC algortihm

Note : Private key is available in application settings of account . Copy it from there.                
                    require 'openssl'
require 'base64'

key = 'a4f48f692d2b4df9acbad7dff60a32a6'
data = '{
  "transaction": {
    "id": "BBZMiscxy",
    "message": "Paid XAF 5,000 to TECH LIMITED, Trans ID MP210603.1234.L06941.",
    "status_code": "TS",
    "airtel_money_id": "MP210603.1234.L06941"
  }
}'
digest = Base64.encode64(OpenSSL::HMAC.digest(OpenSSL::Digest.new('sha256'), key, data)).strip()

print digest
                Code samples

                
                    import hashlib
import hmac
import base64 
import json
data = {
  "transaction": {
    "id": "BBZMiscxy",
    "message": "Paid XAF 5,000 to TECH LIMITED, Trans ID MP210603.1234.L06941.",
    "status_code": "TS",
    "airtel_money_id": "MP210603.1234.L06941"
  }
}
raw = json.dumps(data)
message = bytes(raw, 'utf-8')

secret = bytes('a4f48f692d2b4df9acbad7dff60a32a6', 'utf-8')

signature = base64.b64encode(hmac.new(secret, msg=message,
digestmod=hashlib.sha256).digest())
print(signature)

                    <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/crypto-js/3.1.9-1/crypto-js.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/crypto-js/3.1.9-1/hmac-sha256.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/crypto-js/3.1.9-1/enc-base64.min.js"></script>

<script>
var secret = 'a4f48f692d2b4df9acbad7dff60a32a6';
var data = {
  "transaction": {
    "id": "BBZMiscxy",
    "message": "Paid XAF 5,000 to TECH LIMITED, Trans ID MP210603.1234.L06941.",
    "status_code": "TS",
    "airtel_money_id": "MP210603.1234.L06941"
  }
}
var myJSON = JSON.stringify(data);
  var hash = CryptoJS.HmacSHA256(myJSON, secret);
  var hashInBase64 = CryptoJS.enc.Base64.stringify(hash);
  document.write(hashInBase64);
</script>
                               
                    import javax.crypto.Mac;
import javax.crypto.spec.SecretKeySpec;
import org.apache.commons.codec.binary.Base64;

public class ApiSecurityExample {
  public static void main(String[] args) {
    try {
     String secret = "a4f48f692d2b4df9acbad7dff60a32a6";
     String message = "{"transaction":{"id":"BBZMiscxy","message":"Paid XAF 5,000 to TECH LIMITED, Trans ID MP210603.1234.L06941.","status_code":"TS","airtel_money_id":"MP210603.1234.L06941"}}";

      Mac mac = Mac.getInstance("HmacSHA256");
      SecretKeySpec secretKey = new SecretKeySpec(secret.getBytes(), "HmacSHA256");
      mac.init(secretKey);
      encryptedValue = Base64.encodeBase64String(mac.doFinal(message.getBytes()));
     System.out.println(hash);
    }
    catch (Exception e){
     System.out.println("Error");
    }
   }
}                
                    <?php
// Your code here!
$data = "{"transaction":{"id":"BBZMiscxy","message":"Paid XAF 5,000 to TECH LIMITED, Trans ID MP210603.1234.L06941.","status_code":"TS","airtel_money_id":"MP210603.1234.L06941"}}";
$s = hash_hmac('sha256', $data, 'a4f48f692d2b4df9acbad7dff60a32a6', true);
echo base64_encode($s);
?>

Encryption Keys
This API gets the encyption key.

GET /v1/rsa/encryption-keys

Note: For consuming this API consumer needs to add any of the below products in his account : Collection, Disbursement, Cash-In, Cash-Out, ATM Withdrawl.
Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description

true if no error else false.

Version
2.0
Message Signing
Message signing in requests is a security technique to ensure the authenticity and integrity of data sent between parties. It involves generating a key , creating a payload, calculating a hash of the payload, signing the hash with the generated key, and including the signature in the request. On the server side, the private key is used to verify the signature, ensuring the data hasn't been tampered with. This process safeguards against data manipulation and impersonation during transmission. We use a similar technique to sign our request. Partner needs to encrypt the payload using a randomly generated AES key iv pair . Encrypt the key iv using a RSA key and add both encrypted key and encrypted signature to the request as headers. At the server side we use private RSA key to decrypt the AES key iv and encrypt the payload with this key and iv. We match this generated encrypted payload to the signature received to ensure that there is no change in the payload sent by the partner and payload received.

STEPS :

1. Generate a random AES key of size 256 bits and iv of size 128 bits using AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding algorithm.

2. Generated AES key and iv should be base 64 encoded.
Example - Key(PSw37xtnShLl7zgWn4dLSnf1J5GRhRsOD4OfvJOuLIM=) and iv(gGIhDvCBnSBhBgYiXCyMnw==)

3. Fetch RSA key for the consumer from encryption keys dev portal api.
Example - RSA key will be MIGfMA0GCSqGSIb3DQEBAQUAA4GNADCBiQKBgQCYtZNZnPdBFqUIXoSJlvjhYH5m3Yq8OcK6OzQXxcE8nh7kRCwTvTpnNBIY+Dn1TQa9sq/iGuTfXgJABOzTH0Z4vT6V6vB6VlF4W469iH5u+BRwpV3YXYKm4dr5633UO1XBLdAc2X4aXm51HNkZKg+D0/zGOUFxwCAlYKr92kPHwQIDAQAB

4. Encrypt payload using AES key and iv . This encrypted payload will be sent as header x-signature of the request.
For example payload
{"reference": "1234","subscriber": {"country": "CG","currency": "XAF","msisdn": "752604392"},"transaction": {"amount": "100","country": "CG","currency": "XAF","id": "test_id"}}
will be encrypted to bDOsVGZzbK0P5jO/M5QVMH/qxSmRJLEvIPZCdW6H81xvsZNI6jZej54oBQlHZ38yy63QNeyyYcfEkGJ8f3f15wHWs86V9BCIpHSesS3SrhozE/gGA1fLydeSS26mw0jhyt9XIpabk1RjDH59SfsrkHKU38I5mRlthG/t2qXJck0FhNR64bgOExm9CsuxUlfpsSoW1g81g1u5a4yMFIHhp77f+h3EXNHzcEsdSWhqTho= using key and iv generated in point 2

5. Now concatenate the key and iv separated by a colon(Key:Iv)
Example - PSw37xtnShLl7zgWn4dLSnf1J5GRhRsOD4OfvJOuLIM=:gGIhDvCBnSBhBgYiXCyMnw==

6. Encrypt this key:iv using RSA public key (point 3). This encrypted key:iv will be sent as header x-key of the request
Example - PSw37xtnShLl7zgWn4dLSnf1J5GRhRsOD4OfvJOuLIM=:gGIhDvCBnSBhBgYiXCyMnw== will be encrytped using RSA keyMIGfMA0GCSqGSIb3DQEBAQUAA4GNADCBiQKBgQCYtZNZnPdBFqUIXoSJlvjhYH5m3Yq8OcK6OzQXxcE8nh7kRCwTvTpnNBIY+Dn1TQa9sq/iGuTfXgJABOzTH0Z4vT6V6vB6VlF4W469iH5u+BRwpV3YXYKm4dr5633UO1XBLdAc2X4aXm51HNkZKg+D0/zGOUFxwCAlYKr92kPHwQIDAQAB.
So now x-key will be P0nwxJPVzTHzSbxcVyIi5PlpTRfiScRd4WaeSIUlXWtQwlSb0cesrvsi0jpsIUuPwvU3Zmvc2BVMH5m1873I4qAvFE6zTgHXWUZwRh+poXCXLWwn8NLEYqv9rmSQNkO3yvAYWnP4kR+h7NXrwBBSAoRP5w5Ue6TFDWZQECxikP0=

7. Once we receive the x-key and x-signature, we will decrypt the key:iv pair and encrypt the payload with this key:iv. If the encrypted payload matches x-signature, request will be considered valid else forbidden. Encryption
Version
2.0
Encryption documentation details methods for encrypting sensitive data, including PINs and callbacks, ensuring secure handling and protection against unauthorized access.

Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean.This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Encryption API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team
Error Code	Expected Error Reason	Description
DP02010001000
Error while fetching encryption key	Could not fetch encryption key.
DP02010001001
Successfully fetched encryption key.	Encryption key has been fetched successfully.OverDraft Loans
Overdraft loan is a type of loan that is provided to a consumer (agent / subscriber) when they are making a financial transaction but do not have sufficient balance in their respective wallet(s).

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Apply Loan Callback
This API is to be used by partners to notify Loan Switch the success/failure of the loan application request

POST /overdraft-loans/v1/callback/apply-loan

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response
x-country	header	string	true	Country Code
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token
Request Body
Attribute	Description
st
boolean
mandatory	Status Flag. st will be "true" in case of processing status "PROCESSED/QUEUED" and "false" in case of "REJECTED"
txn_res
object
mandatory	Transaction Response
txn_res.processing_status
string
mandatory	Loan processing status. (Ex: PROCESSED / QUEUED / REJECTED)
txn_res.txn_id
string
mandatory	Unique transaction id of Airtel loan switch
txn_res.auth_code
string
mandatory	Partner Transaction Id
txn_res.partner_response
object
mandatory	Partner response map. Contains partner response fields.
partner_response.msg
string
mandatory	Partner response message.
partner_response.loan_id
string
mandatory	Partner Loan Id
partner_response.loan_amount
number
mandatory	The applied Loan Amount
partner_response.loan_fees
number
mandatory	Fees applied on loan amount
partner_response.outstanding_amount
number
mandatory	Total outstanding amount for user. Sum of existing outstanding loan and fees.
partner_response.due_date
string
mandatory	The day by which Loan should be paid
Response Body
Attribute	Description
status
object
Status
status.response_code
string
Product specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.code
string
Status code, HTTP status code
status.success
boolean
Status of API, True: if API is run successfully, False: if there is any failure (validation, exception, etc)
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action (Success, Failure etc)
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.curl -X POST https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/overdraft-loans/v1/callback/apply-loan

  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',

  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer KHC*****aoE36VUN01jhYSkI8P1'
);
$client = new GuzzleHttpClient();
// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('POST', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/overdraft-loans/v1/callback/apply-loan', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

              OverDraft Loans
Overdraft loan is a type of loan that is provided to a consumer (agent / subscriber) when they are making a financial transaction but do not have sufficient balance in their respective wallet(s).

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Repay Loan Callback
This API is to be used by partners to notify Loan Switch the success/failure of the loan repayment request

POST /overdraft-loans/v1/callback/repayment

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response
x-country	header	string	true	Country Code
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token
Request Body
Attribute	Description
st
boolean
mandatory	Status Flag. st will be "true" in case of processing status "PROCESSED/QUEUED" and "false" in case of "REJECTED"
txn_res
object
mandatory	Transaction response
txn_res.processing_status
string
mandatory	Status of the transaction. It can be PROCESSED / QUEUED / REJECTED
txn_res.txn_id
string
mandatory	Unique transaction id of Airtel loan switch
txn_res.auth_code
string
mandatory	Partner Transaction Id
txn_res.partner_response
object
mandatory	Partner response object
partner_response.msg
string
mandatory	Success or Failure message
partner_response.total_amt_paid
number
mandatory	Total Paid amount (Principle amount paid + Fees Amount Paid)
partner_response.principle_amt_paid
number
mandatory	Total Principle Amount Paid
partner_response.fees_amt_paid
number
mandatory	Total Fees amount Paid
partner_response.user_repay_loan_details
array
mandatory	Loan repayment details array. Contains details of all existing loans of the user
user_repay_loan_details.loan_id
string
mandatory	Partner Loan ID
user_repay_loan_details.total_amt_paid
number
mandatory	Total loan amount repaid for the current transaction
user_repay_loan_details.principle_amt_paid
number
mandatory	Amount deducted from principle loan amount
user_repay_loan_details.fees_amt_paid
number
mandatory	Amount deducted from loan fee amount
user_repay_loan_details.total_outstanding_amt
number
mandatory	Total Outstanding Amount after repayment
user_repay_loan_details.is_cleared
boolean
mandatory	Loan is Cleared or not
Response Body
Attribute	Description
status
object
Status
status.response_code
string
Product specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.code
string
Status code, HTTP status code
status.success
boolean
Status of API, True: if API is run successfully, False: if there is any failure (validation, exception, etc)
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action (Success, Failure etc)
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.                   
                        curl -X POST https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/overdraft-loans/v1/callback/repayment
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'Accept: application/json'
  -H 'x-country: CG'
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer CYBQZUtlmuv8Wpgxzpp8JXRYUzFuukFt'

                    URL obj = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/overdraft-loans/v1/callback/repayment");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("POST");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
  new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
  response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

User Enquiry
GET /standard/v1/users/{msisdn}

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
msisdn	path	string	true	The unique number of the user by which he has registered.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
X-Country	header	string	true	Transaction country.
X-Currency	header	string	true	Transaction currency.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
User enquiry response.
data.first_name
string
First name of the user.
data.grade
string
Grade.
data.is_barred
boolean
Barred.
data.is_pin_set
boolean
Pin set.
data.last_name
string
Last name of the user.
data.msisdn
string
The unique number of the user by which he has registered.
data.registration
object
Registration.
registration.status
string
Registration status.
status
object
Status response.
status.code
string
Status code, HTTP status code.
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action.
status.result_code
string
Application-specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.success
boolean
true if no error else false.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication. Code samples

              KYC
Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the KYC API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team.
Error Code	Expected Error Reason	Description
DP02200000000
Failed	User enquiry is failed.
DP02200000001
Success	User enquiry is successful,
DP02200000002
User Not Found	Invalid MSISDN provided as input.Account
Version
2.0
Always stay ahead with the latest version! Older versions may be deprecated at any time, so for the best experience and support, we recommend using the most up-to-date API. Keep your integration future-proof!

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

                    <?php
require 'vendor/autoload.php';
$headers = array(
  'Accept' => '*/*',
  'X-Country' => 'CG',
  'X-Currency' => 'XAF',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UC*****2w'
);
$client = new GuzzleHttpClient();
// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('GET', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/standard/v2/users/balance', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

                      Account
Version
2.0
Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Account API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team
Error Code	Expected Error Reason	Description
DP02100000000
Failed	Balance enquiry is failed.
DP02100000001
Success	Balance enquiry is successful.
DP02100000002
User Not Found	Invalid MSISDN provided as input.Disbursement-APIs
Version
3.0
Always stay ahead with the latest version! Older versions may be deprecated at any time, so for the best experience and support, we recommend using the most up-to-date API. Keep your integration future-proof!

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Payments
POST /standard/v3/disbursements

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
X-Country	header	string	true	Country of transaction.
X-Currency	header	string	true	Currency of transaction.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization Bearer Token.
x-signature	header	string	false	Encrypted payload.
x-key	header	string	false	Encrypted AES key and iv
Request Body
Attribute	Description
payee
object
mandatory	payee
payee.msisdn
string
mandatory	MSISDN without the country code of the payee to which the payment was credited.
payee.wallet_type
string
mandatory	Type of wallet where the payee wants the money to be credited.
reference
string
mandatory	Reference for service / goods purchased.
pin
string
mandatory	Encrypted four digit numeric pin to be send in a transaction request to complete the payment. To check for encryption Please click here
transaction
object
mandatory	transaction
transaction.amount
number
mandatory	Transaction amount which will be credited to payee's wallet.
transaction.id
string
mandatory	Payer unique transaction id to identify the transaction.
transaction.type
string
mandatory	Transaction type
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
data
data.transaction
object
Disbursement credit transaction.
transaction.reference_id
string
Transaction id generated by payment service.
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM.
transaction.id
string
Transaction id generated by partner.
transaction.status
string
Status of the Transaction.
transaction.message
string
The descriptive message of the transaction.
status
object
status
status.response_code
string
Product specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.code
string
Status code, HTTP status code.
status.success
boolean
True if no error else false.
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.                   
                        curl -X POST https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/standard/v3/disbursements
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'Accept: */*'
  -H 'X-Country: CG'
  -H 'X-Currency: XAF'
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer UCcp1oe*******xCzki2w'
  -H 'x-signature: MGsp*********Ag=='
  -H 'x-key: DVZC*******************NM='

           Disbursement-APIs
Version
3.0
Always stay ahead with the latest version! Older versions may be deprecated at any time, so for the best experience and support, we recommend using the most up-to-date API. Keep your integration future-proof!

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Transaction Enquiry
This api gets the status for external transaction Id.

GET /standard/v3/disbursements/{id}

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
id	path	string	true	Transaction id.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
X-Country	header	string	true	Country of transaction.
X-Currency	header	string	true	Currency of transaction.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
data
data.transaction
object
Disbursement credit transaction.
transaction.reference_id
string
Transaction id generated by payment service.
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM.
transaction.id
string
Transaction id generated by partner.
transaction.status
string
Status of the Transaction
transaction.message
string
The descriptive message of the transaction.
status
object
Status Response.
status.response_code
string
Product specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.code
string
Status code, HTTP status code.
status.success
boolean
True if no error else false.
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.

                Disbursement-APIs
Version
3.0
Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean.\nThis page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Disbursement API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team.
Error Code	Expected Error Reason	Description
DP00900001000
Ambiguous	The transaction is still processing and is in ambiguous state. Please do the transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP00900001001
Success	Transaction is successful.
DP00900001003
Maximum transaction limit reached	Maximum transaction limit reached for the day.
DP00900001004
Invalid Amount	Amount entered is out of range with respect to defined limits.
DP00900001005
Failed	Transaction failed or refused.
DP00900001006
In progress	The transaction is still in progress. Please do the transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP00900001007
Insufficient Funds	Not enough funds in account to complete the transaction.
DP00900001009
Invalid Initiatee	Initiatee of the transaction is invalid.
DP00900001010
User Not Allowed	Payer is not an allowed user.
DP00900001012
Invalid Mobile Numer	Mobile number entered is incorrect
DP00900001013
Transaction Refused	The transaction was refused.
DP00900001015
Transaction not Found	Transaction is not found
DP00900001017
Duplicate Transaction Id	Duplicate Transaction Id.
DP00900001018
Forbidden	X-signature and payload did not match.
DP00900001019
Sender is Barred	Payer is Barred
Cash-In-APIs
Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Payments
This API is used to credits the amount to payee wallet from payer wallet.

POST /standard/v1/cashin/

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
X-Country	header	string	true	Country of transaction.
X-Currency	header	string	true	Currency of transaction.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization Bearer Token.
Request Body
Attribute	Description
subscriber
object
mandatory	Subscriber.Cash-In-APIs
Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Transaction Enquiry
This API gets the transaction status corresponding to the requested External Id.

GET /standard/v1/payments/{id}

Note : airtel_money_id is not generated in case of TIP (Transaction in Progress) and TF (Transaction Failed).This is only generated in case of successful transaction TS(Transaction Success). It is recommended to conduct a transaction inquiry at least one minute after the payment API has been called.
Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
id	path	string	true	Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
X-Country	header	string	true	Transaction country.
X-Currency	header	string	true	Transaction currency.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization Bearer Token
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
Transaction enquiry response.
data.transaction
object
Transaction response.
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM in case of successful transaction TS (Transaction Success).
transaction.id
string
Transaction id generated by TPP / Aggregator / Merchant.
transaction.status
string
Possible Status: TF (Transaction Failed) TS (Transaction Success) TA (Transaction Ambiguous) TIP (Transaction in Progress).
status
object
Status response.
status.code
string
Status code, HTTP status code.
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action.
status.result_code
string
Application specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.success
boolean
true if no error else false.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.Cash-In-APIs
Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Cash-In API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team
Error Code	Expected Error Reason	Description
DP01000001000
Ambiguous	The transaction is still processing and is in ambiguous state. Please do the transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP01000001001
Success	Transaction is successful.
DP01000001002
Incorrect Pin	Incorrect pin has been entered.
DP01000001003
Exceeds withdrawal amount limit(s) / Withdrawal amount limit exceeded	The User has exceeded their wallet allowed transaction limit.
DP01000001004
Invalid Amount	The amount user is trying to transfer is less than the minimum amount allowed.
DP01000001005
Failed	Transaction failed.
DP01000001006
In Process	Transaction in pending state. Please check after sometime.
DP01000001007
Insufficient Funds	User wallet does not have enough money to cover the payable amount.
DP01000001008
User Not Allowed	User is not allowed as a payer.
DP01000001009
Initiatee Invalid	Initiatee is invalid.
DP01000001010
Not Permitted	User not allowed as payer.
DP01000001011
Do Not Honour	Transaction is already completed.
DP01000001012
Invalid Mobile Number	Mobile number entered is incorrect or not registered.
DP01000001013
Failed / Refused	The transaction was either failed or refused.
DP01000001014
Transaction Timed Out	The transaction may be processed or failed due to time out. To know the exact status please do the transaction enquiry.ATM Withdrawal API
Version
2.0
Always stay ahead with the latest version! Older versions may be deprecated at any time, so for the best experience and support, we recommend using the most up-to-date API. Keep your integration future-proof!

Version Highlights:

1. Message signing feature has been introduced in this version for ATM Withdrawl Payment and Refund api. 2. Message signing feature can be enabled or disabled for cashin from the security section of the setting tab in application where ATM Withdrawl api is subscribed and approved.

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Payments
Debit the amount from payer wallet and credit in payee wallet.

POST /atmwithdrawal/v2/payments/

Note: authentication_medium can only be PAYCODE. payer.transfer_type can only be WALLET.
Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
x-country-code	header	string	true	Country of transaction.
x-currency-code	header	string	true	Currency of transaction.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
x-signature	header	string	true	Encrypted payload.
x-key	header	string	true	Encrypted AES key and iv.
Request Body
Attribute	Description
payer
object
mandatory	Withdrawal payer.
payer.transfer_type
string
mandatory	Payer transaction transfer type.
payer.msisdn
string
mandatory	MSISDN without the country code of the payer from which the payment will be debited.
payer.wallet_id
string
optional	wallet Id of the payer from which the payment will be debited.
payer.pay_code
string
mandatory	Pay code.
reference
string
optional	Reference for service / goods purchased.
transaction
object
mandatory	Withdrawal transaction detail.
transaction.amount
string
mandatory	Transaction amount which will be credited to payee's wallet.
transaction.id
string
mandatory	Payer unique transaction id to identify the transaction.
authentication_medium
string
mandatory	Channel type of the transaction.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
Atm withdrawal push response.
data.transaction
object
Transaction.
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM.
transaction.id
string
Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction.
transaction.reference_id
string
Reference for the transaction.
transaction.status
string
Transaction status. Possible values TS, TF, and TIP.
transaction.message
string
Message.
status
object
Status response.
status.code
string
Status code, HTTP status code.
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action.
status.response_code
string
Product specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.success
boolean
true if no error else false.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.Code samples

                    
                        curl -X POST https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/atmwithdrawal/v2/payments/
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'Accept: */*'
  -H 'x-country-code: CG'
  -H 'x-currency-code: XAF'
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer UC*****2w'
  -H 'x-signature: MGsp*********Ag=='
  -H 'x-key: DVZC***********NM='
Code samples

                
                    require 'rest-client'
require 'json'
headers = {
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'Accept' => '*/*',
  'x-country-code' => 'CG',
  'x-currency-code' => 'XAF',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UC*****2w',
  'x-signature' => 'MGsp*********Ag==',
  'x-key' => 'DVZC***********NM='
}
result = RestClient.post 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/atmwithdrawal/v2/payments/',
  params: {
}, headers: headers
p JSON.parse(result)
                
                
Body parameter

                        
                            {
    "payer": {
        "transfer_type": "WALLET",
        "msisdn": "68***10",
        "wallet_id": "68***10",
        "pay_code": "XN****ZC"
    },
    "reference": "Merchant Payment",
    "transaction": {
        "amount": "1000",
        "id": "ATMWD3"
    },
    "authentication_medium": "PAYCODE"
}
                        
                    
200 responses

                        
                            {
    "data": {
        "transaction": {
            "airtel_money_id": "COL***45",
            "id": "ATMWD3",
            "reference_id": "AP***89",
            "status": "TS",
            "message": "Transaction Successful"
        }
    },
    "status": {
        "code": "200",
        "message": "Success",
        "response_code": "DP08700001001",
        "success": true
    }
}

                    import requests
headers = {
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'Accept': '*/*',
  'x-country-code': 'CG',
  'x-currency-code': 'XAF',
  'Authorization': 'Bearer UC*****2w',
  'x-signature': 'MGsp*********Ag==',
  'x-key': 'DVZC***********NM='
}
r = requests.post('https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/atmwithdrawal/v2/payments/',  params={},headers = headers)
print(r.json())

                    const headers = {
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'Accept': '*/*',
  'x-country-code': 'CG',
  'x-currency-code': 'XAF',
  'Authorization': 'Bearer UC*****2w',
  'x-signature': 'MGsp*********Ag==',
  'x-key': 'DVZC***********NM='
};
fetch('https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/atmwithdrawal/v2/payments/',{
  method: 'POST',
  body: inputBody,
  headers: headers
}).then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

                    URL obj = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/atmwithdrawal/v2/payments/");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("POST");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
  new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
  response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

                    <?php
require 'vendor/autoload.php';
$headers = array(
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'Accept' => '*/*',
  'x-country-code' => 'CG',
  'x-currency-code' => 'XAF',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UC*****2w',
  'x-signature' => 'MGsp*********Ag==',
  'x-key' => 'DVZC***********NM='
);
$client = new GuzzleHttpClient();
// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('POST', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/atmwithdrawal/v2/payments/', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

   ATM Withdrawal API
Version
2.0
Always stay ahead with the latest version! Older versions may be deprecated at any time, so for the best experience and support, we recommend using the most up-to-date API. Keep your integration future-proof!

Version Highlights:

1. Message signing feature has been introduced in this version for ATM Withdrawl Payment and Refund api. 2. Message signing feature can be enabled or disabled for cashin from the security section of the setting tab in application where ATM Withdrawl api is subscribed and approved.

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Refund
Refund the amount from payee wallet and credit in payer wallet.

POST /atmwithdrawal/v2/payments/refund/

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
x-country-code	header	string	true	Country of transaction.
x-currency-code	header	string	true	Currency of transaction.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization Bearer token
x-signature	header	string	true	Encrypted payload.
x-key	header	string	true	Encrypted AES key and iv.
Request Body
Attribute	Description
transaction
object
mandatory	Transaction.
transaction.id
string
mandatory	Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
Payment refund response.
data.transaction
object
Refund transaction.
transaction.reference_id
string
Reference for the transaction.
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM.
transaction.id
string
Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction.
transaction.message
string
Message.
transaction.status
string
Transaction status. Possible values TS, TF, and TIP.
status
object
Status response.
status.response_code
string
Application specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.code
string
Status code, HTTP status code.
status.success
boolean
true if no error else false.
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.               
                    require 'rest-client'
require 'json'
headers = {
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'Accept' => '*/*',
  'x-country-code' => 'CG',
  'x-currency-code' => 'XAF',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UC*****2w',
  'x-signature ' => 'MGsp*********Ag==',
  'x-key ' => 'DVZC***********NM='
}
result = RestClient.post 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/atmwithdrawal/v2/payments/refund/',
  params: {
}, headers: headers
p JSON.parse(result)
                
                
Body parameter

                        
                            {
    "transaction": {
        "id": "ATMWD3"
    }
}
                        
                    
200 responses

                        
                            {
    "data": {
        "transaction": {
            "reference_id": "AP***84",
            "airtel_money_id": "COL***45",
            "id": "ATMWD3",
            "message": "Transaction Reversed",
            "status": "TS"
        }
    },
    "status": {
        "response_code": "DP08700001001",
        "code": "200",
        "success": true,
        "message": "Your transaction has been successfully processed."
    }
}

    'headers' => $headers,

  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

                    <?php
require 'vendor/autoload.php';
$headers = array(
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UC*****2w',
  'x-country-code' => 'CG',
  'x-currency-code' => 'XAF'
);
$client = new GuzzleHttpClient();
// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('GET', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/atmwithdrawal/v1/payments/{transaction_id}?authentication_medium=PAYCODE', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

}ATM Withdrawal API
Version
2.0
Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the ATM Withdrawl API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team.
Error Code	Expected Error Reason	Description
DP08700001000
Ambiguous	The transaction is still processing and is in ambiguous state. Please do the transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP08700001001
Success	Transaction is successful.
DP08700001003
Limit Exceeded	User Exceeds withdrawal transaction limit.
DP08700001004
Invalid Amount	User enters invalid amount.
DP08700001005
Failed	User has dropped in between the transaction.
DP08700001006
In Process	User is in between the process. Transaction in process.
DP08700001007
Insufficient Funds	User has insufficient funds to complete the transaction.
DP08700001008
User Not Allowed	User is not allowed.
DP08700001009
Invalid Initiatee	Initiatee is invalid.
DP08700001010
Not Permitted	User not allowed as payer.
DP08700001011
Do Not Honour	Transaction is already completed.
DP08700001012
Invalid Mobile Number	Invalid mobile number.
DP08700001013
Failed / Refused	The transaction was either failed or refused.
DP08700001014
Transaction Not Allowed	Transaction is not allowed.
DP08700001015
Bad Request	Bad request.
DP08700001016
Transaction Already Exist	This transaction already exists.
DP08700001017
Invalid Paycode	User entered a wrong paycode.
DP08700001018
Paycode Already Used	User entered a paycode which was already used.
DP08700001019
Incorrect MSISDN	User entered a wrong MSISDN.
DP08700001020
Incorrect Currency	User entered wrong currency.
DP08700001021
Incorrect Account / Wallet Details	User entered incorrect account details.
DP08700001022
Incorrect Merchant Code	User entered a wrong merchant code.
DP08700001023Transactions-Summary-APIs
Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Transactions Summary
API will be used to retrieve transactions details of merchant's MSISDN for a defined period.

GET /merchant/v1/transactions

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
x-country	header	string	true	Country of transaction.
x-currency	header	string	true	Currency of transaction.
from	query	integer	true	Date (in EPOCH format) from which transactions are to be fetched.
to	query	integer	true	Date (in EPOCH format) until transactions are to be fetched.
limit	query	integer	true	The number of transactions to be fetched on a page.
offset	query	integer	true	Page number from which transactions are to be fetched.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
Transactions response.
data.count
integer
The number of transactions returned in the response.
data.transactions
object
Reference for the transaction.
transactions.charges
object
Charges.
charges.service
string
Service charge amount.
transactions.payee
object
Payee.
payee.currency
string
The currency in which the payee's wallet was credited.
payee.msisdn
string
MSISDN without the country code of the payee to which the payment was credited.
payee.name
string
Name of the payee to which the payment was credited.
transactions.payer
object
Payer.
payer.currency
string
The currency in which the payer's wallet was debited.
payer.msisdn
string
MSISDN without the country code of the payer from which the payment was debited.
payer.name
string
Name of the payer from which the payment was debited.
transactions.service
object
Service.
service.type
string
A service type of transaction. Possible values are MERCHPAY, CASHIN.
transactions.transaction
object
Transaction
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM.
transaction.amount
string
The amount which was debited from the payer wallet and credited to the payee wallet.
transaction.created_at
number
The timestamp for the transaction initiation.
transaction.id
number
Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction.
transaction.reference_number
string
Reference for the transaction.
transaction.status
string
Transaction status. Possible values TS, TF, and TIP.
status
object
Status.
status.code
number
Status code, HTTP status code.
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action.
status.result_code
string
Application specific code to identify the error and success response. This will be different for the type of error and success.
status.success
boolean
true if no error else false.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.                   
                        curl -X GET https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant/v1/transactions

                    URL obj = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant/v1/transactions");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
  new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
  response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

require 'vendor/autoload.php';
$headers = array(
  'Accept' => '*/*',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UCc*****ki2w',
  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'x-currency' => 'XAF'
);
$client = new GuzzleHttpClient();
// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('GET', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant/v1/transactions', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

          RemX
Overview
The RemX suite enables seamless electronic fund transfers across borders, empowering individuals and organizations to send money internationally. It connects various financial institutions and payment service providers, streamlining the remittance process—especially for transactions from non-Airtel ecosystems to Airtel subscribers.

Product Architecture
Flow: Kong Gateway → MFS Platform (API Layer) → MQ (Transaction Processing)

Partners & Use Cases
Currently, both products support transactions from non-Airtel partners (e.g., Thunes, TerraPay) to Airtel subscribers only. Partners can integrate with the Developer Portal APIs to facilitate international remittances to Airtel customers.

Credit: Transfers an amount from a Payer account to a payee account.
Validate: Verifies if a customer is active and able to receive funds.
Refund: Makes a full refund to partners.
Status: Checks the status of the transaction.
Integration & Validation Process
Authorization & Token Generation
Authenticate using your client_id and client_secret to generate an Authorization Token.
API Used: Token Generation (OAuth)
Note: The token is valid for 180 seconds and must be refreshed upon expiry.

KYC (Know Your Customer) Verification
Validate the payee’s KYC details before transferring funds.
API Used: Validate API
Purpose: Ensures the recipient’s wallet is legitimate and active, reducing fraud risk.

Initiating the Transfer
Once the payee is verified and the balance is sufficient, initiate the transfer.
API Used: Credit API
Details Required: Payer and payee wallet information, transfer amount, and related data.

Response Handling
Process the system’s response to the transfer request, which may indicate success, failure, in progress, or an ambiguous state.

Transaction Enquiry
Query the status of a transaction after initiation to check completion or troubleshoot issues.
API Used: Status API

Refunds
Initiate a full refund if required.
API Used: Refund API
Error Handling and Categorization
The Remittance APIs facilitate the electronic transfer of funds across financial institutions and payment service providers, handling international remittances from non-Airtel partners to Airtel subscribers. Throughout the transaction process, robust error handling ensures that both partners and end users are informed of issues promptly and accurately.

Remittance transactions typically involve deducting funds from a partner’s account and crediting the recipient’s Airtel wallet. The process includes multiple steps—such as KYC validation, balance checks, and transfer initiation—each of which may encounter specific error scenarios.

If any issues arise at any stage, the APIs return standardized error codes and messages to indicate the cause of failure. Common error situations include:

Insufficient funds in the partner’s account
Invalid or incomplete KYC details for the recipient
Issues with recipient wallet
Each API response includes an error code and a descriptive message to help partners identify and resolve issues quickly. A list of error codes and their meanings is available in the API documentation, allowing partners to implement precise and user-friendly error handling within their platforms.

By following the prescribed integration steps and handling API responses as documented, partners can ensure seamless and secure remittance experiences for their users.

Flow Diagram

{figure1}

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

User Enquiry API
This Api can be used to do a validation if a customer is active and able to receive funds.

POST /imt/v1/validate

Note: This will only validate that the customer is available and active. It does not validate the amount.
Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
x-country	header	string	true	Transaction Country
x-currency	header	string	true	Transaction Currency
Request Body
Attribute	Description
payee
object
mandatory	payee object
payee.identifier_type
string
mandatory	Type of the identifier
payee.identifier_code
string
mandatory	Value of the identifier
amount
string
optional	Amount to be transacted
Response Body
Attribute	Description
status
object
status object
status.success
boolean
True if no error else false
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action
status.code
string
HTTP status code
status.response_code
string
Application-specific code to identify the error and success response.
data
object
data object
data.first_name
string
Receiver's first name
data.last_name
string
Receiver's last name
data.msisdn
string
Receiver's msisdn
data.business_name
string
Receiver's business name
data.full_name
string
Receiver's fullname
data.user_type
string
Type of the user
data.nickname
string
Receiver's Nickname
data.account_status
string
Receiver's account status: Y(Active), N(Inactive).
data.pin_set
string
True if receiver's AM pin is set else false.
data.user_barred
string
True if receiver is barred else false.
data.bar_type
string
Block type of the user
data.aml_status
string
Receiver's eligibility to receive the given amount.
data.message
string
Message for KYC
data.status
string
Status Code
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.Code samples

  -H 'x-currency: XAF'

                    const inputBody = {
    "payee":{
        "identifier_type": "nickname",
        "identifier_code": "10352096"
    },
    "amount": "10"
};
const headers = {
  'Authorization': 'Bearer 10OvEtYZto0QserLGtpekQjeAAadwgaT',
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'x-country': 'CG',
  'x-currency': 'XAF'
};
fetch('https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/validate', {
  method: 'POST',
  body: inputBody,
  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
  if (!res.ok) { throw new Error('Request failed with status ' + res.status); }
  return res.json();
})
.then(function(body) {
  console.log(body);
})
.catch(function(error) {
  console.error('Error:', error);
});

   Code samples

                
                    URL url = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/validate");
HttpURLConnection connection = (HttpURLConnection) url.openConnection();
connection.setRequestMethod("POST");
connection.setRequestProperty("Content-Type", "application/json");
connection.setDoOutput(true);
JSONObject body = new JSONObject();
JSONObject payee = new JSONObject();
payee.put("identifier_type", "nickname");
payee.put("identifier_code", "10352096");
body.put("payee", payee);
body.put("amount", "10");
DataOutputStream wr = new DataOutputStream(connection.getOutputStream());
wr.writeBytes(body.toString());
wr.flush();
wr.close();
int responseCode = connection.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(connection.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

                    <?php
// Define headers array
$headers = array(
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer 10OvEtYZto0QserLGtpekQjeAAadwgaT',
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'x-currency' => 'XAF',
);

// Prepare cURL request
$ch = curl_init();
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_URL, 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/validate');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST, 'POST');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
// Prepare the request body
$request_body = json_decode('{
    "payee":{
        "identifier_type": "nickname",
        "identifier_code": "10352096"
    },
    "amount": "10"
}', true);
$post_data = json_encode($request_body, JSON_UNESCAPED_SLASHES);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, $post_data);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, array(
  'Content-Type: application/json',
  
));
// Execute the cURL request
$response = curl_exec($ch);
if (curl_errno($ch)) {
  echo 'Error:' . curl_error($ch);
}
curl_close($ch);
print_r($response);
?>

           RemX
Overview
The RemX suite enables seamless electronic fund transfers across borders, empowering individuals and organizations to send money internationally. It connects various financial institutions and payment service providers, streamlining the remittance process—especially for transactions from non-Airtel ecosystems to Airtel subscribers.

Product Architecture
Flow: Kong Gateway → MFS Platform (API Layer) → MQ (Transaction Processing)

Partners & Use Cases
Currently, both products support transactions from non-Airtel partners (e.g., Thunes, TerraPay) to Airtel subscribers only. Partners can integrate with the Developer Portal APIs to facilitate international remittances to Airtel customers.

Credit: Transfers an amount from a Payer account to a payee account.
Validate: Verifies if a customer is active and able to receive funds.
Refund: Makes a full refund to partners.
Status: Checks the status of the transaction.
Integration & Validation Process
Authorization & Token Generation
Authenticate using your client_id and client_secret to generate an Authorization Token.
API Used: Token Generation (OAuth)
Note: The token is valid for 180 seconds and must be refreshed upon expiry.

KYC (Know Your Customer) Verification
Validate the payee’s KYC details before transferring funds.
API Used: Validate API
Purpose: Ensures the recipient’s wallet is legitimate and active, reducing fraud risk.

Initiating the Transfer
Once the payee is verified and the balance is sufficient, initiate the transfer.
API Used: Credit API
Details Required: Payer and payee wallet information, transfer amount, and related data.

Response Handling
Process the system’s response to the transfer request, which may indicate success, failure, in progress, or an ambiguous state.

Transaction Enquiry
Query the status of a transaction after initiation to check completion or troubleshoot issues.
API Used: Status API

Refunds
Initiate a full refund if required.
API Used: Refund API
Error Handling and Categorization
The Remittance APIs facilitate the electronic transfer of funds across financial institutions and payment service providers, handling international remittances from non-Airtel partners to Airtel subscribers. Throughout the transaction process, robust error handling ensures that both partners and end users are informed of issues promptly and accurately.

Remittance transactions typically involve deducting funds from a partner’s account and crediting the recipient’s Airtel wallet. The process includes multiple steps—such as KYC validation, balance checks, and transfer initiation—each of which may encounter specific error scenarios.

If any issues arise at any stage, the APIs return standardized error codes and messages to indicate the cause of failure. Common error situations include:

Insufficient funds in the partner’s account
Invalid or incomplete KYC details for the recipient
Issues with recipient wallet
Each API response includes an error code and a descriptive message to help partners identify and resolve issues quickly. A list of error codes and their meanings is available in the API documentation, allowing partners to implement precise and user-friendly error handling within their platforms.

By following the prescribed integration steps and handling API responses as documented, partners can ensure seamless and secure remittance experiences for their users.

Flow Diagram

{figure1}

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Payment API
This API is used to transfer an amount from a Payer account to a payee account.

POST /imt/v1/credit

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
x-country	header	string	true	x-country
x-currency	header	string	true	x-currency
Request Body
Attribute	Description
payee
object
mandatory	payee object
payee.msisdn
string
mandatory	Receiver's msisdn
transaction
object
mandatory	transaction object
transaction.amount
number
mandatory	Amount to be transacted
transaction.id
string
mandatory	Transaction id generated at remittance partner for the transaction. Must be unique for each transaction
transaction.forex_rate
string
mandatory	Forex rate
payer
object
mandatory	payer object
payer.country
string
mandatory	Payer country
payer.first_name
string
mandatory	Payer first name
payer.last_name
string
mandatory	Payer last name
payer.nationality
string
optional	Payer nationality
payer.amount
number
optional	Amount in payer's currency
payer.currency
string
optional	Payer's currency
payer.id_number
string
optional	Payer's id number
payer.address
string
optional	Payer's address
additional_info
object
optional	additional_info object
additional_info.sector_code
string
optional	Sector for the purpose of the transaction
additional_info.purpose_code
string
optional	Purpose of transaction
additional_info.source_of_fund
string
optional	Source of funds
additional_info.transaction_source
string
optional	Source of Transaction
pin
string
mandatory	Encrypted four digit numeric pin to be send in a transaction request to complete the payment
txn_type
string
mandatory	Transaction type
Response Body
Attribute	Description
status
object
status object
status.success
boolean
True if no error else false
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action
status.code
string
HTTP status code
status.response_code
string
Application-specific code to identify the error and success response
data
object
data object
data.transaction
object
transaction object
data.transaction.id
string
Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction
data.transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM
data.transaction.reference_id
string
Reference for the transaction
data.transaction.status
string
Transaction status
data.transaction.message
string
Message
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.Code samples

                    
                        curl -X POST https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/credit
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer UCc*****ki2w'
  -H 'Accept: */*'
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'x-country: CG'
  -H 'x-currency: XAF'
  --data '{
    "payee": {
        "msisdn": "756255985"
    },
    "transaction": {
        "amount": 100,
        "id": "1747047503",
        "forex_rate": "0.408171"
    },
    "payer": {
        "country": "ZM",
        "first_name": "Bob",
        "last_name": "Builder",
        "nationality": "Indian",
        "amount": 200,
        "currency": "ZMW",
        "id_number": "987345",
        "address": "PLOT 197865 Lusaka, Nyumba Yanga"
    },
    "additional_info": {
        "sector_code": "T9700",
        "purpose_code": "10001",
        "source_of_fund": "Salary",
        "transaction_source": "Bank Transfer"
    },
    "pin": "cNydw=",
    "txn_type": "MERCHANT_PAYMENT"
}'

            "reference_id": "APCCG173533944374656",
            "status": "TS",
            "message": "Transaction Successful"
        }

  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UCc*****ki2w',
  'Accept' => '*/*',
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'x-currency' => 'XAF'
}
payload = {
    "payee": {
        "msisdn": "756255985"
    },
    "transaction": {
        "amount": 100,
        "id": "1747047503",
        "forex_rate": "0.408171"
    },
    "payer": {
        "country": "ZM",
        "first_name": "Bob",
        "last_name": "Builder",
        "nationality": "Indian",
        "amount": 200,
        "currency": "ZMW",

        "source_of_fund": "Salary",
        "transaction_source": "Bank Transfer"
    },
    "pin": "cNydw=",
    "txn_type": "MERCHANT_PAYMENT"
};
result = RestClient.post 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/credit',
  payload.to_json,
  headers: headers
p JSON.parse(result)
