        Code samples

                
                    const inputBody = {
    "payee": {
        "msisdn": "756255985"
    },
    "transaction": {
        "amount": 100,
        "id": "1747047503",
        "forex_rate": "0.408171"
    },
    "payer": {
        "country": "ZM",
        "first_name": "Bob",
        "last_name": "Builder",
        "nationality": "Indian",
        "amount": 200,
        "currency": "ZMW",
        "id_number": "987345",
        "address": "PLOT 197865 Lusaka, Nyumba Yanga"
    },
    "additional_info": {
        "sector_code": "T9700",
        "purpose_code": "10001",
        "source_of_fund": "Salary",
        "transaction_source": "Bank Transfer"
    },
    "pin": "cNydw=",
    "txn_type": "MERCHANT_PAYMENT"
};
const headers = {
  'Authorization': 'Bearer UCc*****ki2w',
  'Accept': '*/*',
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'x-country': 'CG',
  'x-currency': 'XAF'
};
fetch('https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/credit', {
  method: 'POST',
  body: inputBody,
  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
  if (!res.ok) { throw new Error('Request failed with status ' + res.status); }
  return res.json();
})
.then(function(body) {
  console.log(body);
})
.catch(function(error) {
  console.error('Error:', error);
});

                Code samples

                
                    URL url = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/credit");
HttpURLConnection connection = (HttpURLConnection) url.openConnection();
connection.setRequestMethod("POST");
connection.setRequestProperty("Content-Type", "application/json");
connection.setDoOutput(true);
JSONObject body = new JSONObject();
JSONObject payee = new JSONObject();
payee.put("msisdn", "756255985");
body.put("payee", payee);
JSONObject transaction = new JSONObject();
transaction.put("amount", "100");
transaction.put("id", "1747047503");
transaction.put("forex_rate", "0.408171");
body.put("transaction", transaction);
JSONObject payer = new JSONObject();
payer.put("country", "ZM");
payer.put("first_name", "Bob");
payer.put("last_name", "Builder");
payer.put("nationality", "Indian");
payer.put("amount", "200");
payer.put("currency", "ZMW");
payer.put("id_number", "987345");
payer.put("address", "PLOT 197865 Lusaka, Nyumba Yanga");
body.put("payer", payer);
JSONObject additional_info = new JSONObject();
additional_info.put("sector_code", "T9700");
additional_info.put("purpose_code", "10001");
additional_info.put("source_of_fund", "Salary");
additional_info.put("transaction_source", "Bank Transfer");
body.put("additional_info", additional_info);
body.put("pin", "cNydw=");
body.put("txn_type", "MERCHANT_PAYMENT");
DataOutputStream wr = new DataOutputStream(connection.getOutputStream());
wr.writeBytes(body.toString());
wr.flush();
wr.close();
int responseCode = connection.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(connection.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

                    URL url = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/credit");
HttpURLConnection connection = (HttpURLConnection) url.openConnection();
connection.setRequestMethod("POST");
connection.setRequestProperty("Content-Type", "application/json");
connection.setDoOutput(true);
JSONObject body = new JSONObject();
JSONObject payee = new JSONObject();
payee.put("msisdn", "756255985");
body.put("payee", payee);
JSONObject transaction = new JSONObject();
transaction.put("amount", "100");
transaction.put("id", "1747047503");
transaction.put("forex_rate", "0.408171");
body.put("transaction", transaction);
JSONObject payer = new JSONObject();
payer.put("country", "ZM");
payer.put("first_name", "Bob");
payer.put("last_name", "Builder");
payer.put("nationality", "Indian");
payer.put("amount", "200");
payer.put("currency", "ZMW");
payer.put("id_number", "987345");
payer.put("address", "PLOT 197865 Lusaka, Nyumba Yanga");
body.put("payer", payer);
JSONObject additional_info = new JSONObject();
additional_info.put("sector_code", "T9700");
additional_info.put("purpose_code", "10001");
additional_info.put("source_of_fund", "Salary");
additional_info.put("transaction_source", "Bank Transfer");
body.put("additional_info", additional_info);
body.put("pin", "cNydw=");
body.put("txn_type", "MERCHANT_PAYMENT");
DataOutputStream wr = new DataOutputStream(connection.getOutputStream());
wr.writeBytes(body.toString());
wr.flush();
wr.close();
int responseCode = connection.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(connection.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

            PHP Code samples

                
                    <?php
// Define headers array
$headers = array(
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UCc*****ki2w',
  'Accept' => '*/*',
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'x-currency' => 'XAF',
);

// Prepare cURL request
$ch = curl_init();
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_URL, 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/credit');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST, 'POST');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
// Prepare the request body
$request_body = json_decode('{
    "payee": {
        "msisdn": "756255985"
    },
    "transaction": {
        "amount": 100,
        "id": "1747047503",
        "forex_rate": "0.408171"
    },
    "payer": {
        "country": "ZM",
        "first_name": "Bob",
        "last_name": "Builder",
        "nationality": "Indian",
        "amount": 200,
        "currency": "ZMW",
        "id_number": "987345",
        "address": "PLOT 197865 Lusaka, Nyumba Yanga"
    },
    "additional_info": {
        "sector_code": "T9700",
        "purpose_code": "10001",
        "source_of_fund": "Salary",
        "transaction_source": "Bank Transfer"
    },
    "pin": "cNydw=",
    "txn_type": "MERCHANT_PAYMENT"
}', true);
$post_data = json_encode($request_body, JSON_UNESCAPED_SLASHES);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, $post_data);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, array(
  'Content-Type: application/json',
  
));
// Execute the cURL request
$response = curl_exec($ch);
if (curl_errno($ch)) {
  echo 'Error:' . curl_error($ch);
}
curl_close($ch);
print_r($response);
?>

        "success": true,
        "message": "SUCCESS",
        "code": "200",
        "response_code": "DP00900001001"
    },
    "data": {
        "transaction": {
            "id": "STDREMXTEST5",
            "airtel_money_id": "REMX-ASBDGHKL-STDREMXTEST5",
            "reference_id": "APCCG173533944374656",
            "status": "TS",
            "message": "Transaction Successful"
        }
    }
}
                 RemX
Overview
The RemX suite enables seamless electronic fund transfers across borders, empowering individuals and organizations to send money internationally. It connects various financial institutions and payment service providers, streamlining the remittance process—especially for transactions from non-Airtel ecosystems to Airtel subscribers.

Product Architecture
Flow: Kong Gateway → MFS Platform (API Layer) → MQ (Transaction Processing)

Partners & Use Cases
Currently, both products support transactions from non-Airtel partners (e.g., Thunes, TerraPay) to Airtel subscribers only. Partners can integrate with the Developer Portal APIs to facilitate international remittances to Airtel customers.

Credit: Transfers an amount from a Payer account to a payee account.
Validate: Verifies if a customer is active and able to receive funds.
Refund: Makes a full refund to partners.
Status: Checks the status of the transaction.
Integration & Validation Process
Authorization & Token Generation
Authenticate using your client_id and client_secret to generate an Authorization Token.
API Used: Token Generation (OAuth)
Note: The token is valid for 180 seconds and must be refreshed upon expiry.

KYC (Know Your Customer) Verification
Validate the payee’s KYC details before transferring funds.
API Used: Validate API
Purpose: Ensures the recipient’s wallet is legitimate and active, reducing fraud risk.

Initiating the Transfer
Once the payee is verified and the balance is sufficient, initiate the transfer.
API Used: Credit API
Details Required: Payer and payee wallet information, transfer amount, and related data.

Response Handling
Process the system’s response to the transfer request, which may indicate success, failure, in progress, or an ambiguous state.

Transaction Enquiry
Query the status of a transaction after initiation to check completion or troubleshoot issues.
API Used: Status API

Refunds
Initiate a full refund if required.
API Used: Refund API
Error Handling and Categorization
The Remittance APIs facilitate the electronic transfer of funds across financial institutions and payment service providers, handling international remittances from non-Airtel partners to Airtel subscribers. Throughout the transaction process, robust error handling ensures that both partners and end users are informed of issues promptly and accurately.

Remittance transactions typically involve deducting funds from a partner’s account and crediting the recipient’s Airtel wallet. The process includes multiple steps—such as KYC validation, balance checks, and transfer initiation—each of which may encounter specific error scenarios.

If any issues arise at any stage, the APIs return standardized error codes and messages to indicate the cause of failure. Common error situations include:

Insufficient funds in the partner’s account
Invalid or incomplete KYC details for the recipient
Issues with recipient wallet
Each API response includes an error code and a descriptive message to help partners identify and resolve issues quickly. A list of error codes and their meanings is available in the API documentation, allowing partners to implement precise and user-friendly error handling within their platforms.

By following the prescribed integration steps and handling API responses as documented, partners can ensure seamless and secure remittance experiences for their users.

Flow Diagram

{figure1}

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Check Status API
This API gets the transaction status corresponding to the requested External Id.

GET /imt/v1/checkstatus/{id}

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request
x-country	header	string	true	x-country
x-currency	header	string	true	x-currency
id	path	string	true	Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction
Response Body
Attribute	Description
status
object
status object
status.success
boolean
True if no error else false
status.message
string
The descriptive message of the response/action
status.code
string
HTTP status code
status.response_code
string
Application-specific code to identify the error and success response
data
object
data object
data.transaction
object
transaction object
data.transaction.id
string
Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction
data.transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM
data.transaction.reference_id
string
Reference for the transaction
data.transaction.status
string
Transaction status
data.transaction.message
string
Message
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.Shell                     
                        curl -X GET https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/checkstatus/{id}
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer UCc*****ki2w'
  -H 'Accept: */*'
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'x-country: CG'
  -H 'x-currency: XAF'

            "id": "STDREMXTEST5",

            "reference_id": "APCCG173533944374656",
            "status": "TS",
            "message": "Transaction Successful"
        }
    }

} Javascript Code samples

                
                    const headers = {
  'Authorization': 'Bearer UCc*****ki2w',
  'Accept': '*/*',
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'x-country': 'CG',
  'x-currency': 'XAF'
};
fetch('https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/checkstatus/{id}', {
  method: 'GET',
  headers: headers
})
.then(function(res) {
  if (!res.ok) { throw new Error('Request failed with status ' + res.status); }
  return res.json();
})
.then(function(body) {
  console.log(body);
})
.catch(function(error) {
  console.error('Error:', error);
});

            Java                 
                    URL url = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/checkstatus/{id}");
HttpURLConnection connection = (HttpURLConnection) url.openConnection();
connection.setRequestMethod("GET");
connection.setRequestProperty("Content-Type", "application/json");
int responseCode = connection.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(connection.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

                    <?php
// Define headers array
$headers = array(
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UCc*****ki2w',
  'Accept' => '*/*',
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'x-currency' => 'XAF',
);

// Prepare cURL request
$ch = curl_init();
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_URL, 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/checkstatus/{id}');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, array(
  'Content-Type: application/json',
  'id: 175437***5'
));
// Execute the cURL request
$response = curl_exec($ch);
if (curl_errno($ch)) {
  echo 'Error:' . curl_error($ch);
}
curl_close($ch);
print_r($response);
?>

Refund API
This API is used to make full refunds to Partners.

POST /imt/v1/refund

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request
x-country	header	string	true	x-country
x-currency	header	string	true	x-currency
Request Body
Attribute	Description
transaction
object
mandatory	transaction object
transaction.id
string
mandatory	Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction
pin
string
mandatory	Encrypted four digit numeric pin to be send in a transaction request to complete the payment
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
data object
data.transaction
object
transaction object
data.transaction.id
string
Partner unique transaction id to identify the transaction
data.transaction.airtel_money_id
string
Transaction id generated by AM
data.transaction.message
string
Message
reference_id
string
Reference for the transaction
data.transaction.status

Transaction status

object
status object

string
HTTP status code

boolean

         Java Code samples

                
                    URL url = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/refund");
HttpURLConnection connection = (HttpURLConnection) url.openConnection();
connection.setRequestMethod("POST");
connection.setRequestProperty("Content-Type", "application/json");
connection.setDoOutput(true);
JSONObject body = new JSONObject();
JSONObject transaction = new JSONObject();
transaction.put("id", "1753167656");
body.put("transaction", transaction);
body.put("pin", "d***lQ=");
DataOutputStream wr = new DataOutputStream(connection.getOutputStream());
wr.writeBytes(body.toString());
wr.flush();
wr.close();
int responseCode = connection.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(connection.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
    response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

            PHP                
                    <?php
// Define headers array
$headers = array(
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UCc*****ki2w',
  'Accept' => '*/*',
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'x-currency' => 'XAF',
);

// Prepare cURL request
$ch = curl_init();
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_URL, 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/imt/v1/refund');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST, 'POST');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
// Prepare the request body
$request_body = json_decode('{
    "transaction": {
        "id": "1753167656"
    },
    "pin": "d***lQ="
}', true);
$post_data = json_encode($request_body, JSON_UNESCAPED_SLASHES);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, $post_data);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, array(
  'Content-Type: application/json',
  
));
// Execute the cURL request
$response = curl_exec($ch);
if (curl_errno($ch)) {
  echo 'Error:' . curl_error($ch);
}
curl_close($ch);
print_r($response);
?>

Flow Diagram

{figure1}

Error Codes
Error Code	Expected Error Reason	Description
DP03570001000
Ambiguous	The transaction is still processing and is in ambiguous state. Please do the transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP03570001001
Success	Transaction is successful.
DP03570001002
User Enquiry Failed	User enquiry failed or user not found.
DP03570001003
Bad Request	Bad request or invalid input.
DP03570001004
Invalid Amount	User enters invalid amount.
DP03570001005
User has dropped in between the transaction	User has dropped in between the transaction
DP03570001006
Transaction In Progress	User is in between the process. Transaction in process.
DP03570001007
Insufficient Funds	User has insufficient funds to complete the transaction.
DP03570001009
Withdrawal transaction limit exceeded	Withdrawal transaction limit exceeded.
DP03570001010
Something Went Wrong	Unknown or unclassified error or technical error.
DP03570001011
Maximum Transaction Limit Reached	Maximum transaction Limit reached.
DP03570001012
Transaction Failed	Transaction Failed
DP03570001013
Invalid Payer or Payee	Invalid Payer or Payee
DP03570001014
Invalid mobile number	Invalid mobile number
DP03570001015
Transaction Not Found	Transaction Not Found
DP03570001016
Duplicate transaction ID	Duplicate transaction ID
DP03570001017
MSISDN Barred / MSISDN locked	MSISDN Barred / MSISDN locked
DP03570001018
Transaction Timed out	Transaction Timed out
DP03570001019
Invalid Amount	Amount entered is less than the minimum allowed value
DP03570001020
Maximum amount allowed for the day limit reached	Maximum amount allowed for the day limit reached Billers Callback
Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Billers Callback
Partner will update the status of async customer initiated transaction, based on which transaction will be marked as SUCCESS or FAILURE.

POST /arch-in/web/callback/billers/{partnerCode}

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
partnerCode	path	string	true	Partner code
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
x-country	header	string	true	Transaction country.
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
Request Body
Attribute	Description
txnRes
object
mandatory	Transaction response.
txnRes.authCode
string
mandatory	Partner transaction id.
txnRes.id
string
mandatory	Airtel money transaction id.
txnRes.partnerResponse
object
optional	Object for sending additional values.
txnRes.processingStatus
string
mandatory	Transaction Status. Example: PROCESSED, REJECTED.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
msg
string
Message description.
msgid
string
Message id.
st
boolean
Status.
timestamp
string
Response timestamp.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication. Shell                    
                        curl -X POST https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/arch-in/web/callback/billers/{partnerCode}
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'Accept: application/json'
  -H 'x-country: CG'
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer  UC****2w'


                    
                
Body parameter

                        
                            {
    "txnRes": {
        "authCode": "AB***56",
        "id": "ab***1z",
        "partnerResponse": {},
        "processingStatus": "PROCESSED"
    }
}
                        
                    
200 responses

                        
                            {
  "msg": "SUCCESS",
  "msgid": "SPINE_200",
  "st": true,
  "timestamp": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z"
}

}

  params: {
}, headers: headers
p JSON.parse(result)

            Javascript                
                    const headers = {
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'Accept': 'application/json',
  'x-country': 'CG',
  'Authorization': 'Bearer  UC****2w'
};
fetch('https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/arch-in/web/callback/billers/{partnerCode}',{
  method: 'POST',
  body: inputBody,
  headers: headers
}).then(function(res) {
    return res.json();
}).then(function(body) {
    console.log(body);
});

        Java Code samples

                
                    URL obj = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/arch-in/web/callback/billers/{partnerCode}");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("POST");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
  new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
  response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

           PHP                 
                    <?php
require 'vendor/autoload.php';
$headers = array(
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'Accept' => 'application/json',
  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer  UC****2w'
);
$client = new GuzzleHttpClient();
// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('POST', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/arch-in/web/callback/billers/{partnerCode}', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

} Loan User KYC
Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

KYC
This API is used to fetch KYC Information of Users based on alias or msisdn.

POST /arch-in/web/callback/loans/user-kyc/{partnerCode}

This version will be deprecated soon. Please migrate to version 2.0.
Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
partnerCode	path	string	true	Partner code
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the response.
x-country	header	string	true	Country Code
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
Request Body
Attribute	Description
alias
string
optional	Hashed msisdn of User for which KYC is requested. Either msisdn or alias field should be present.
msisdn
string
optional	Msisdn of User for which KYC is requested. Either msisdn or alias field should be present.
partnerCode
string
optional	The partner code, who is requesting for KYC
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
Response data.
data.entityName
string
Represents the Business name of the merchant/agent or full name of individual in case of subscribers.
data.firstName
string
Represents the first name of an individual (agent/subscriber). Can be ignored for merchants.
data.hashedMsisdn
string
Hashed msisdn of User for which KYC is requested.
data.lastName
string
Represents the last name of an individual (agent/subscriber). Can be ignored for merchants.
data.msisdn
string
Customer's MSISDN as is.
msg
string
Status message
msgid
string
Response code
st
boolean
Status Flag
timestamp
string
Current Timestamp
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.Shell                    
                        curl -X POST https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/arch-in/web/callback/loans/user-kyc/{partnerCode}


  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'Accept: */*'
  -H 'x-country: CG'
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer UCc*****ki2w'

                            {
    "alias": "78****70",
    "msisdn": "123****90",
    "partnerCode": "airtel_rw"
}

    "data": {
        "entityName": "USER XYZ",
        "firstName": "USER",
        "hashedMsisdn": "78c2****e70",
        "lastName": "XYZ",
        "msisdn": "123***90"
    },
    "msg": "string",
    "msgid": "string",
    "st": true,
    "timestamp": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z"
}

                    Bank to Wallet
Content Type
JSON
Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Payment
This API facilitates banks to transfer funds from a bank account to airtel user's AM wallet

POST /bank-to-wallet/v1/payments/

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Content-Type	header	string	true	Specifies the media type of the request.
Accept	header	string	true	The "Accept" header is an HTTP header that specifies the media types that the client can understand or process
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
x-country	header	string	true	Transaction country.
x-currency	header	string	true	Transaction currency.
Request Body
Attribute	Description
transaction
object
mandatory	Information about the bank to wallet transaction.
transaction.id
string
mandatory	A unique identifier for the transaction.
transaction.amount
string
mandatory	The amount of money involved in the transaction.
transaction.payee
object
mandatory	Information about the recipient of the transaction.
payee.address_type
string
mandatory	Specifies the type of address used for the payee. Supported value is MOBILE
payee.msisdn
string
mandatory	MSISDN of the payee.
note
string
mandatory	Additional remarks or comments related to the transaction.
additional_info
object
optional	This field is reserved for future use. It allows for passing any additional fields that may be introduced in future versions of the system without altering the existing structure. Any extra details not covered by the predefined parameters can be included here.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
Contains the data related to the transaction.
data.additional_info
object
extra details not covered by the predefined parameters can be included here.
additional_info.mq_txn_id
string
Mobiquity Transaction Id
data.transaction
object
Information about the transaction.
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
The ID associated with the transaction in Airtel Money system.
transaction.reference_id
string
A reference ID associated with the transaction.
transaction.id
string
The unique identifier for the transaction sent in the request.
transaction.status
string
Status of the Transaction
transaction.message
string
A message providing information about the status of the transaction.
status
object
Contains the status of the response.
status.response_code
string
Product specific codes are used to identify both error and success responses, and these codes will vary depending on the type of error or success encountered.
status.code
string
An HTTP status code.
status.success
boolean
true if no error else false.
status.message
string
A message providing information about the status of the response.
This operation requires Oauth2 authentication. Shell Code samples

                    
                        curl -X POST https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/bank-to-wallet/v1/payments/
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'Accept: application/json'
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer UCLcp1oeq44KPXr8X*******xCzki2w'
  -H 'x-country: CG'
  -H 'x-currency: XAF'


                    
                
Body parameter

                        
                            {
    "transaction": {
        "id": "AB********ya",
        "amount": "100",
        "payee": {
            "address_type": "MOBILE",
            "msisdn": "1********0"
        }
    },
    "note": "Bank initiated bank to wallet transfer",
    "additional_info": {}
}
                        
                    
200 responses

                        
                            {
    "data": {
        "additional_info": {
            "mq_txn_id": "BW24**4.0**3.G**015"
        },
        "transaction": {
            "airtel_money_id": "product-partner-**41",
            "reference_id": "APC**4",
            "id": "AB***141",
            "status": "TS",
            "message": "Transaction Successful"
        }
    },
    "status": {
        "response_code": "DP02100001001",
        "code": "200",
        "success": true,
        "message": "success"
    }
}

               PHP                
                    <?php
require 'vendor/autoload.php';
$headers = array(
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'Accept' => 'application/json',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UCLcp1oeq44KPXr8X*******xCzki2w',
  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'x-currency' => 'XAF'
);
$client = new GuzzleHttpClient();
// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('POST', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/bank-to-wallet/v1/payments/', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

            Bank to Wallet
Content Type
JSON
Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Transaction Enquiry
This api can be used to fetch transaction status

GET /bank-to-wallet/v1/payments/{id}

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
id	path	string	true	A unique identifier for the transaction.
Accept	header	string	true	The "Accept" header is an HTTP header that specifies the media types that the client can understand or process
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization bearer token.
x-country	header	string	true	Transaction country.
x-currency	header	string	true	Transaction currency.
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
Contains the data related to the transaction.
data.additional_info
object
extra details not covered by the predefined parameters can be included here.
additional_info.mq_txn_id
string
Mobiquity transaction Id
data.transaction
object
Information about the transaction.
transaction.airtel_money_id
string
The ID associated with the transaction in Airtel Money system.
transaction.reference_id
string
A reference ID associated with the transaction.
transaction.id
string
The unique identifier for the transaction sent in the request.
transaction.status
string
Status of the Transaction
transaction.message
string
A message providing information about the status of the transaction.
status
object
Contains the status of the response.
status.response_code
string
Product specific codes are used to identify both error and success responses, and these codes will vary depending on the type of error or success encountered.
status.code
string
An HTTP status code.
status.success
boolean
true if no error else false.
status.message
string
A message providing information about the status of the response.
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication.Code samples

                    
                        curl -X GET https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/bank-to-wallet/v1/payments/{id}
  -H 'Accept: application/json'
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer UCLcp1oeq44KPXr8X*******xCzki2w'
  -H 'x-country: CG'
  -H 'x-currency: XAF'

                       Java Code samples

                
                    URL obj = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/bank-to-wallet/v1/payments/{id}");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
  new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
  response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

               Bank to Wallet
Content Type
JSON
Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Bank to Wallet API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team
Error Code	Expected Error Reason	Description
DP02100001000
Ambiguous	The transaction is still processing or is in ambiguous state. Please perform transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP02100001001
Success	Transaction is successful.
DP02100001002
Duplicate Transaction ID	Duplicate Transaction ID. Please use a unique transaction id.
DP02100001003
Transaction Time Out	Transaction Timed Out. Please perform transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP02100001004
Transaction Not Found	Transaction is not found . Please verify the transaction id and enquire again.
DP02100001005
Failed	Transaction failed or refused.
DP02100001006
Transaction In Progress	The transaction is still in progress. Please perform a transaction enquiry to fetch the transaction status.
DP02100001007
Invalid Input	One of the input entered is invalid.MERCHANT COLLECTION
Merchant collection requires the Merchant to register their micro-merchants on airtel through dev portal . They can now use merchant collection product to pull funds from airtel subscribers to their micro-merchant wallet.

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Register Merchant
This API is to register a submerchant of a particular merchant

POST /merchant-collection/v1/merchant

Accept	header	string	true	Accept

merchants

mandatory	merchants

             MERCHANT COLLECTION
Merchant collection requires the Merchant to register their micro-merchants on airtel through dev portal . They can now use merchant collection product to pull funds from airtel subscribers to their micro-merchant wallet.

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Fetch registered merchant
This API is used to fetch the registered micro merchant

GET /merchant-collection/v1/fetch

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
x-country	header	string	true	x-country
x-currency	header	string	true	x-currency
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization
Content-Type	header	string	true	Content-Type
Accept	header	string	true	Accept
relationship_id	query	string	true	Aggregator assigned unique id for its merchant

data
object

                    Java Code samples

                
                    URL obj = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant-collection/v1/fetch?relationship_id=Test123");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("GET");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
  new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
  response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

                  PHP Code samples

                
                    <?php
require 'vendor/autoload.php';
$headers = array(
  'x-country' => 'CG',
  'x-currency' => 'XAF',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer j3cvwSYMvH5prycoDY8Wm0Zu39iip1UB',
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'Accept' => 'application/json'
);
$client = new GuzzleHttpClient();
// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('GET', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant-collection/v1/fetch?relationship_id=Test123', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

        MERCHANT COLLECTION
Merchant collection requires the Merchant to register their micro-merchants on airtel through dev portal . They can now use merchant collection product to pull funds from airtel subscribers to their micro-merchant wallet.

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Transfers
This API is used to initiate the transaction

POST /merchant-collection/v1/payments

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
x-country	header	string	true	x-country
x-currency	header	string	true	x-currency
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization
Content-Type	header	string	true	Content-Type
Accept	header	string	true	Accept
Request Body
Attribute	Description
payee
object
mandatory	Payee.
payee.relationship_id
string
mandatory	Aggregator assigned unique id for its merchant. Recommended to use 6 length.
payer
object
mandatory	Payer.
payer.msisdn
string
mandatory	Payer msisdn.
transaction
object
mandatory	Transaction object.
transaction.id
string
mandatory	Unique Id for transaction.
transaction.amount
string
mandatory	Amount to be transffered.
transaction.reference
string
mandatory	Reference for the transaction to identify payment in transaction reports. max allowed length is 25.
additional_info
object
optional	Additional info regarding transactions can be passed in this json block
Response Body
Attribute	Description
data
object
data
data.transaction
object
data.transaction
data.transaction.id
string
data.transaction.id
data.transaction.airtel_money_id
string
data.transaction.airtel_money_id
data.transaction.status
string
data.transaction.status
data.transaction.message
string
data.transaction.message
data.additional_info
object
data.additional_info
status
object
status
status.success
boolean
status.success
status.message
string
status.message
status.code
string
status.code
status.response_code
string
status.response_code
This operation does require Oauth2 authentication. Shell                    
                        curl -X POST https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant-collection/v1/payments
  -H 'x-country: CG'
  -H 'x-currency: XAF'
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer j3cvwSYMvH5prycoDY8Wm0Zu39iip1UB'
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
  -H 'Accept: application/json'


                    
                
Body parameter

                        
                            {
    "payee": {
        "relationship_id": "Test123"
    },
    "payer": {
        "msisdn": "123****89"
    },
    "transaction": {
        "id": "TEST1",
        "amount": "1000",
        "reference": "Fine Works Test Transaction"
    },
    "additional_info": {}
}
                        
                    
200 responses

                        
                            {
    "data": {
        "transaction": {
            "id": "TEST1",
            "airtel_money_id": "ACOLL-ABCDEFGH-PQRSTU-TEST1",
            "status": "TIP",
            "message": "Transaction is in progress."
        },
        "additional_info": {
        }
 
    },
    "status": {
        "success": true,
        "message": "Success",
        "code": "200",
        "response_code": "DP03520001001"
    }

Parameters

           MERCHANT COLLECTION
Merchant collection requires the Merchant to register their micro-merchants on airtel through dev portal . They can now use merchant collection product to pull funds from airtel subscribers to their micro-merchant wallet.

Base URLs:

Staging -- https://openapiuat.airtel.cg

Production -- https://openapi.airtel.cg

Refund Payment
This API is used to initiate refund process for any complete transaction

POST /merchant-collection/v1/payments/refund

Parameters
Name	In	Type	Required	Description
Accept	header	string	true	Accept
Content-Type	header	string	true	Content-Type
X-Country	header	string	true	X-Country
X-Currency	header	string	true	X-Currency
Authorization	header	string	true	Authorization
Request Body
Attribute	Description
transaction

                    URL obj = new URL("https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant-collection/v1/payments/refund");
HttpURLConnection con = (HttpURLConnection) obj.openConnection();
con.setRequestMethod("POST");
int responseCode = con.getResponseCode();
BufferedReader in = new BufferedReader(
  new InputStreamReader(con.getInputStream()));
String inputLine;
StringBuffer response = new StringBuffer();
while ((inputLine = in.readLine()) != null) {
  response.append(inputLine);
}
in.close();
System.out.println(response.toString());

                    <?php
require 'vendor/autoload.php';
$headers = array(
  'Accept' => '*/* ',
  'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
  'X-Country' => 'CG',
  'X-Currency' => 'XAF',
  'Authorization' => 'Bearer UC*******2w'
);
$client = new GuzzleHttpClient();
// Define array of request body.
$request_body = array();
try {
  $response = $client->request('POST', 'https://openapiuat.airtel.cg/merchant-collection/v1/payments/refund', array(
    'headers' => $headers,
    'json' => $request_body
    )
  );
 print_r($response->getBody()->getContents());
}
catch (GuzzleHttpExceptionBadResponseException $e) {
// handle exception or api errors.
  print_r($e->getMessage());
}

                   Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Platforms API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team

Common Error Codes
Code	Description	
ROUTER001	Wallet is not configured -- Your Application Wallet is not configured. So we cannot process your request.	
ROUTER003	Missing header/body params -- The request failed because mandatory request header or body is missing.	
ROUTER005	Country route not configured -- The request failed due to country route is not configured. Please contact customer support.	
ROUTER006	Invalid Country -- The request failed due to incorrect value of country code in the request	
ROUTER007	User Not authorized -- You are not authorized to perform any operations in the provided country. So we cannot process your request.	
ROUTER112	Invalid Currency -- The request failed due to incorrect currency code in the request	
ROUTER114	Error while Validating Pin -- The request failed due to error occured while validating Pin in the request.	
ROUTER115	Incorrect Pin -- The request failed due to incorrect pin in the request	
ROUTER116	Incorrect Encrypted Pin -- The encrypted value of the PIN is incorrect. Please review the encryption mechanism. The request has failed.	
ROUTER117	Request Timeout -- The request has timed out. In case of payments/refund, please perform a transaction enquiry; otherwise, try again later.	
ROUTER119	Invalid/Missing Currency -- The request failed due to an invalid or missing currency in the request parameters.Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Platforms API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team

Product Specific Error Codes
Code	Description	
ESB000001	Something went wrong. The request can be in ambiguous state please do a transaction enquiry.	
ESB000004	An error occurred while initiating the payment. The request can be in ambiguous state please do a transaction enquiry.	
ESB000008	Field validation Error. The request can be in ambiguous state please do a transaction enquiry.	
ESB000010	Request was Success.	
ESB000011	Request was Failed.	
ESB000014	An error occurred while fetching the transaction status. The request can be in ambiguous state please do a transaction enquiry after some time.	
ESB000033	The request has been failed due to Invalid MSISDN Length. Please try again with correct request.	
ESB000034	The request has been failed due to Invalid Country Name. Please try again with correct request.	
ESB000035	The request has been failed due to Invalid Currency Code. Please try again with correct request.	
ESB000036	The request has been failed due to either it is Invalid MSISDN Length or it doesn't starts with 0. Please try again with correct request.	
ESB000039	Vendor is not configured to do transaction in the provided country. Please try again with correct request.	
ESB000041	Transaction with External Transaction ID already exists. Please try again with correct request.	
ESB000045	No Transaction Found With Provided Transaction Id.	
0000900	Transaction might be in ambigous state. Please check response_code to know the exact status or perform a transaction enquiry.Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Platforms API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team

HTTP Error Codes
Code	Description	
400	Bad Request -- Your request is invalid.	
401	Unauthorized -- Your API key or bearer token is incorrect.	
403	Forbidden -- The requested item is hidden for administrators only.	
404	Not Found -- The specified path could not be found.	
405	Method Not Allowed -- You tried to access a path with an invalid method.	
408	Read Timeout -- The request has timed out. In case of payments/refund, please perform an transaction enquiry; otherwise, try again later.	
429	Too Many Requests -- You're requesting too many requests! Slow down.	
500	Internal Server Error -- We had a problem with our server. Try again later.	
503	Service Unavailable -- We're temporarily offline for maintenance. Please try again later.	
502	Bad gateway -- In case of payments/refund, please perform an transaction enquiry; otherwise, try again later.	
504	Gateway Timeout -- In case of payments/refund, please perform an transaction enquiry; otherwise, try again later.Error Codes
Learn about error codes and what they mean. This page contains codes and descriptions of all errors that may occur when you communicate with the Platforms API. If after reading this section you are still not sure how to interpret a specific error, contact the support team

Other solutions

Remittance

The MoMo Remittance API enables secure, cross-border money transfers via the MTN Mobile Money platform. It allows licensed remittance providers to send funds directly to a recipient's MoMo wallet in supported countries.



Distribute

We enable businesses to distribute MTN and MoMo services and facilitate cash in, cash out and airtime sales and get commission from MTN and MoMo.



Channel as a Service

Our Interact API enables businesses to expose and manage a sub menu within the MoMo USSD and App channels. This enables businesses to make their service visible to the millions of consumers and businesses who use MoMo products



KYC/ Account Validation

We enable businesses to get KYC information on MoMo Consumers so they don't have to collect it from consumers. This simplifies customer onboarding for businesses.



Pre-approval

We enable businesses to use the Momo Pin to contract with their customers using the Momo Pin to accept T&Cs.



Notify

We enable businesses to send a customised SMS to MoMo Consumers and Business within the Pay or Get Paid transaction.

View All APIs MoMo API

Brand Guidelines

Introduction

               English

               French

             

The purpose of this site is to detail the design principles, objects, behaviours and error handling for the MTN Mobile Money API. The overriding goal of the API is to enable all parties to implement MTN Mobile Money APIs in a flexible, yet consistent manner. We hope to achieve this by the implementing the following principles:



Use of REST architectural principles.

Providing a set of well-defined objects that are abstracted from the underlying object representations held in the various mobile money systems. This allows an API client to construct an API message without requiring specific knowledge of the target server implementation.

Creation of a standard set of transaction types and other key enumerations, removing the need for developers to map for each and every API implementation.

Use of ISO international standards for enumerators such as currency and country codes

Use of supplementary metadata and sub-types to enable use case and/or mobile money provider-specific properties to be conveyed where necessary.

Recognising that no common mobile money account identifier exists, use of a flexible construct to enable the target account(s) and transaction parties to be identified using one or multiple identifier types.

The Open API is a JSON REST API that is used by Partner systems to access services in the Wallet platform. The Open API exposes services that are used by e.g. online merchants for managing payments and other financial services. This document gives an overview of the structure of the API.

Brand Guidelines

Introduction

               English

               French

             

L'objectif de ce site est de detailler les principes de conception, les objets, les comportements et la gestion des erreurs pour l'API MTN Mobile Money. L'objectif principal de l'API est de permettre a toutes les parties d'implementer les API MTN Mobile Money de maniere flexible, tout en garantissant la coherence. Nous esperons atteindre cet objectif en appliquant les principes suivants :



Utilisation des principes d'architecture REST.

Fourniture d'un ensemble d'objets bien definis, abstraits des representations d'objets sous-jacentes presentes dans les differents systemes de mobile money. Cela permet a un client API de construire un message API sans necessiter de connaissance specifique de l'implementation du serveur cible.

Creation d'un ensemble standard de types de transactions et d'autres enumerations cles, supprimant ainsi la necessite pour les developpeurs de faire des correspondances pour chaque implementation d'API.

Utilisation des normes internationales ISO pour les enumerations telles que les codes de devise et les codes pays.

Utilisation de metadonnees supplementaires et de sous-types afin de permettre la transmission, lorsque necessaire, de proprietes specifiques aux cas d'usage et/ou aux fournisseurs de mobile money.

Reconnaissance de l'absence d'un identifiant commun pour les comptes de mobile money, et utilisation d'une structure flexible permettant d'identifier le(s) compte(s) cible(s) et les parties a la transaction a l'aide d'un ou de plusieurs types d'identifiants.

L'Open API est une API REST basee sur JSON, utilisee par les systemes partenaires pour acceder aux services de la plateforme Wallet. L'Open API expose des services utilises, par exemple, par les commercants en ligne pour la gestion des paiements et d'autres services financiers. Ce document fournit une vue d'ensemble de la structure de l'API.

Getting Started

English
y'ello, in this section we'll walk you through getting up and running on our Mobile Money Open API. Here you will:

Signup For An Account

Subscribe To Our Products

Manage Your Subscriptions

Generate API User and API Key

1. Signup For An Account

An account activation link will be sent in an email. The activation link expires within 24 hours of it being sent, and you will need to register for another account.

2. Subscribe To Our Products

On the products page on our developer portal you should see 4 items you can subscribe to:

Collection Widget

Collection

Remittances

Disbursements

Each product will have a brief description, a link to the corresponding documentation and Subscribe button on the Products page.


3. Manage Your Subscriptions

Developers are issued a Primary Key and Secondary Key for every product.


Both primary and secondary Subscription key provides access to the API. Without one of them a developer cannot access any of the APIs. Subscriptions are stored under the user profile and have no expiry.

Here you can view the status of the package, date it started, conduct cancellation or activation actions, and also show or regenerate your Primary Key and Secondary Key


4. Generate API User and API Key

You are now almost ready to start we building with our Mobile Money Open API. The next thing we need to do is to Provision the API User and API Key using the Sandbox Provisioning API. We do this in the next section.

Getting Started

French
y'ello, dans cette section, nous vous guiderons pour demarrer avec notre Mobile Money Open API. Ici, vous pourrez :

Creer un compte

Souscrire a nos produits

Gerer vos souscriptions

Generer API User et API Key

1. Creer un compte

Un lien d'activation de compte sera envoye par email. Ce lien expire dans un delai de 24 heures apres son envoi, et vous devrez creer un nouveau compte si le lien expire.

2. Souscrire a nos produits

Sur la page des produits de notre portail developpeur, vous devriez voir 4 elements auxquels vous pouvez souscrire :

Collection Widget

Collection

Remittances

Disbursements

Chaque produit contient une breve description, un lien vers la documentation correspondante, ainsi qu'un bouton "Subscribe" sur la page des produits.


3. Gerer vos souscriptions


Les developpeurs recoivent une Primary Key et une Secondary Key pour chaque produit.

La Primary Key et la Secondary Key permettent toutes deux d'acceder a l'API. Sans l'une de ces cles, un developpeur ne peut acceder a aucune API. Les souscriptions sont enregistrees dans le profil utilisateur et n'ont pas de date d'expiration.

Ici, vous pouvez voir le statut du package, la date de debut, effectuer des actions d'annulation ou d'activation, ainsi qu'afficher ou regenerer votre Primary Key et votre Secondary Key.


4. Generer API User et API Key

Vous etes maintenant presque pret a commencer a construire avec notre Mobile Money Open API. La prochaine etape consiste a provisionner l'API User et l'API Key en utilisant la Sandbox Provisioning API. Cette etape est detaillee dans la section suivante.

Brand Guidelines
API User and Key Management

English
Authentication

There are two credentials used in the Open API.

Subscription Key

API User and API Key

The subscription key is used to give access to APIs in the API Manager portal. A user is assigned a subscription Key as and when the user subscribes to products in the API Manager Portal.

The API User and API Key are used to grant access to the wallet system in a specific country. API user and Key are wholly managed by the user through Partner Portal.

Users are allowed to generate/revoke API Keys through the Partner Portal.

However, on Sandbox Environment a Provisioning API is exposed to enable users to generate their own API User and API Key for testing purposes only.

Subscription Key

The subscription key is part of the header of all requests sent to the API Manager. The subscription key can be found under user profile in the API Manager Portal.

The subscription key is assigned to the Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key parameter of the header.

API User and API Key for Oauth 2.0

The API user and API key are provisioned differently in the sandbox and production environment.

In the Sandbox a provisioning API is used to create the API User and API Key, whereas in the production environment the provisioning is done through the User Portal.

The sections below describe the different steps required in creating API User and API key in Sandbox and Production Environments.

Create API User


The Provider sends a POST {baseURL}/apiuser request to Wallet platform.

The Provider specifies the UUID Reference ID in the request Header and the subscription Key

Reference ID will be used as the User ID for the API user to be created.

Wallet Platform creates the User and responds with 201

Example

Request
POST {baseURL}/apiuser HTTP/1.1

Host: momodeveloper.mtn.com

X-Reference-Id: c72025f5-5cd1-4630-99e4-8ba4722fad56

Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key: d484a1f0d34f4301916d0f2c9e9106a2{"providerCallbackHost": "clinic.com"}

Response
201 Created

Create API Key


The Provider sends a POST {baseURL}/apiuser/{APIUser}/apikey request to Wallet platform.

The Provider specifies the API User in the URL and subscription Key in the header.

Wallet Platform creates the API Key and responds with 201 Created with the newly Created API Key in the Body.
 
Provider now has both API User and API Key created.

Example

Request
POST {baseURL}/apiuser/c72025f5-5cd1-4630-99e4-8ba4722fad56/apikey HTTP/1.1

Host: momodeveloper.mtn.com

Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key: d484a1f0d34f4301916d0f2c9e9106a2

Response
HTTP/1.1 201 Created date: Wed, 10 Oct 2018 09:16:15 GMT content-type: application/json;charset=utf-8 content-length: 45 { "apiKey": "f1db798c98df4bcf83b538175893bbf0" }

GET API User Details

It is possible to fetch API user details such as Call Back Host. However, it is not possible to fetch the API key.Provider shall be required to generate a new Key should they lose the existing one.


The Provider sends a GET {baseURL}/apiuser/{APIUser} request to Wallet platform.

The Provider specifies the API User in the URL and subscription Key in the header.

Wallet Platform responds with 200 Ok and details of the user.

TargetEnvironment is preconfigured to sandbox in the Sandbox environment, therefore Providers will not have the option of setting it to a different parameter.

Example

Request
GET {baseURL}/apiuser/ c72025f5-5cd1-4630-99e4-8ba4722fad56

Host: momodeveloper.mtn.com

Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key: d484a1f0d34f4301916d0f2c9e9106a2

Response
HTTP/1.1 200 Accepted date: Wed, 10 Oct 2018 09:16:15 GMT { "providerCallbackHost": "clinic.com", "targetEnvironment": "sandbox" }

Oauth 2.0

The Open API uses Oauth 2.0 token for authentication of request. User will request an access token using Client Credential Grant according to RFC 6749. The token received is according to RFC 6750 Bearer Token.

The API user and API key are used in the basic authentication header when requesting the access token. The API user and key are managed in the Partner GUI for the country where the account is located. The Partner can create and manage API user and key from the Partner GUI.

In the Sandbox, the API Key and API User are managed through the Provisioning API

The received token has an expiry time. The same token can be used for transactions until it expires. A new token is requested by using the POST /token service in the same way as the initial token. The new token can be requested for before the previous one has expired to avoid authentication failure due to expired token.

Important: The token must be treated as a credential and kept secret. The party that have access to the token will be authenticated as the user that requested the token. The below sequence describes the flow for requesting a token and using the token in a request.


Provider system requests an access token using the API Key and API user as authentication.

Wallet platform authenticates credentials and responds with the access token

Provider system will use the access token for any request that is sent to Wallet Platform, e.g. POST /requesttopay

Note: The same token shall be used if it is not expired.

API Methods

The API uses POST, GET, PUT methods. This section gives an overview of the interaction sequence used in the API and the usage of the methods.

POST
POST method is used for creating a resource in Wallet Platform. The request includes a reference id which is used to uniquely identify the specific resource that are created by the POST request. If a POST is using a reference id that is already used, then a duplication error response will be sent to the client.

Example

POST /requesttopay

The POST is an asynchronous method. The Wallet Platform will validate the request to ensure that it is correct according to the API specification and then answer with HTTP 202 Accepted. The created resource will get status PENDING. Once the request has been processed the status will be updated to SUCCESSFUL or FAILED. The requester may then be notified of the final status through callback.

GET
Example

GET is used for requesting information about a specific resource. The URL in the GET shall include the reference of the resource. If a resource was created with POST then the reference id that was provided in the request is used as the identity of the resource.

POST /requesttopay request is sent with X-Reference-Id = 11377cbe-374c-43f6-a019-4fb70e57b617

GET /requesttopay/11377cbe-374c-43f6-a019-4fb70e57b617 will return the status of the request.

PUT
The PUT method is used by the Open API when sending callbacks. Callback is sent if a callback URL is included in the POST request. The Wallet Platform will only send the callback once. There is no retry on the callback if the Partner system does not respond. If the callback is not received, then the Partner system can use GET to validate the status.

API User and Key Management

French
Authentication

Il existe deux types d'identifiants utilises dans l'Open API :

Subscription Key

API User et API Key

La subscription key est utilisee pour donner acces aux API dans le portail API Manager. Un utilisateur recoit une subscription key lorsqu'il souscrit a des produits dans le portail API Manager.

L'API User et l'API Key sont utilises pour donner acces au systeme Wallet dans un pays specifique. L'API User et l'API Key sont entierement geres par l'utilisateur via le Partner Portal.

Les utilisateurs peuvent generer ou revoquer des API Keys via le Partner Portal.

Cependant, dans l'environnement Sandbox, une Provisioning API est exposee afin de permettre aux utilisateurs de generer leur propre API User et API Key a des fins de test uniquement.

Subscription Key

La subscription key fait partie du header de toutes les requetes envoyees a l'API Manager. Elle peut etre trouvee dans le profil utilisateur du portail API Manager.

La subscription key est assignee au parametre de header Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key.

API User and API Key for Oauth 2.0

L'API User et l'API Key sont provisionnes differemment selon l'environnement Sandbox ou Production.

Dans le Sandbox, une Provisioning API est utilisee pour creer l'API User et l'API Key, tandis qu'en environnement Production, le provisioning est effectue via le User Portal.

Les sections ci-dessous decrivent les differentes etapes necessaires a la creation de l'API User et de l'API Key dans les environnements Sandbox et Production.

Creer un API User


Le Provider envoie une requete POST {baseURL}/apiuser a la plateforme Wallet.

Le Provider specifie le UUID Reference ID dans le header de la requete ainsi que la subscription key.

Le Reference ID sera utilise comme User ID pour l'API user a creer.

La plateforme Wallet cree l'utilisateur et repond avec 201.

Exemple

Requete
POST {baseURL}/apiuser HTTP/1.1

Host: momodeveloper.mtn.com

X-Reference-Id: c72025f5-5cd1-4630-99e4-8ba4722fad56

Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key: d484a1f0d34f4301916d0f2c9e9106a2{"providerCallbackHost": "clinic.com"}

Reponse
201 Created

Creer une API Key


Le Provider envoie une requete POST {baseURL}/apiuser/{APIUser}/apikey a la plateforme Wallet.

Le Provider specifie l'API User dans l'URL et la subscription Key dans le header.

La plateforme Wallet cree l'API Key et repond avec 201 Created avec la nouvelle API Key dans le Body.

Le Provider dispose maintenant d'un API User et d'une API Key.

Exemple

Requete
POST {baseURL}/apiuser/c72025f5-5cd1-4630-99e4-8ba4722fad56/apikey HTTP/1.1

Host: momodeveloper.mtn.com

Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key: d484a1f0d34f4301916d0f2c9e9106a2

Reponse
HTTP/1.1 201 Created date: Wed, 10 Oct 2018 09:16:15 GMT content-type: application/json;charset=utf-8 content-length: 45 { "apiKey": "f1db798c98df4bcf83b538175893bbf0" }

Recuperer les details de l'API User

Il est possible de recuperer les details de l'API user tels que le Call Back Host. Cependant, il n'est pas possible de recuperer l'API key. Provider devra generer une nouvelle Key s'il perd celle existante.


Le Provider envoie une requete GET {baseURL}/apiuser/{APIUser} a la plateforme Wallet.

Le Provider specifie l'API User dans l'URL et la subscription Key dans le header.

La plateforme Wallet repond avec 200 Ok et les details de l'utilisateur.

Le TargetEnvironment est preconfigure a sandbox dans l'environnement Sandbox, par consequent les Providers ne peuvent pas le definir sur une autre valeur.

Exemple

Requete
GET {baseURL}/apiuser/ c72025f5-5cd1-4630-99e4-8ba4722fad56

Host: momodeveloper.mtn.com

Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key: d484a1f0d34f4301916d0f2c9e9106a2

Reponse
HTTP/1.1 200 Accepted date: Wed, 10 Oct 2018 09:16:15 GMT { "providerCallbackHost": "clinic.com", "targetEnvironment": "sandbox" }

Oauth 2.0

L'Open API utilise un token Oauth 2.0 pour l'authentification des requetes. L'utilisateur demandera un access token en utilisant le Client Credential Grant conformement au RFC 6749. Le token recu est un Bearer Token conformement au RFC 6750.

L'API user et l'API key sont utilises dans le header d'authentification basic lors de la demande du token. L'API user et la key sont geres dans le Partner GUI pour le pays ou le compte est localise. Le Partner peut creer et gerer l'API user et la key depuis le Partner GUI.

Dans le Sandbox, l'API Key et l'API User sont geres via la Provisioning API.

Le token recu a une duree d'expiration. Le meme token peut etre utilise pour les transactions jusqu'a expiration. Un nouveau token est demande via le service POST /token de la meme maniere que le token initial. Un nouveau token peut etre demande avant expiration pour eviter un echec d'authentification.

Important : Le token doit etre traite comme un identifiant et garde secret. Toute partie ayant acces au token sera authentifiee comme l'utilisateur ayant demande le token. La sequence ci-dessous decrit le flux d'obtention et d'utilisation du token.


Le systeme Provider demande un access token en utilisant l'API Key et l'API user pour l'authentification.

La plateforme Wallet authentifie les identifiants et repond avec un access token

Le systeme Provider utilisera le access token pour toute requete envoyee a la plateforme Wallet, par exemple POST /requesttopay

Note : Le meme token doit etre utilise s'il n'est pas expire.

Methodes API

L'API utilise les methodes POST, GET et PUT. Cette section donne un apercu de la sequence d'interaction utilisee dans l'API ainsi que de l'utilisation des methodes.

POST
La methode POST est utilisee pour creer une ressource dans la plateforme Wallet. La requete inclut un reference id qui identifie de maniere unique la ressource. Si un reference id deja utilise est envoye, une erreur de duplication sera retournee.

Exemple

POST /requesttopay

La methode POST est asynchrone. La plateforme Wallet valide la requete et repond avec HTTP 202 Accepted. La ressource creee aura le statut PENDING. Une fois traitee, le statut sera mis a jour vers SUCCESSFUL ou FAILED. Le demandeur pourra etre notifie via callback.

GET
Exemple

GET est utilise pour demander des informations sur une ressource specifique. L'URL doit inclure le reference de la ressource. Si la ressource a ete creee via POST, le reference id est utilise comme identifiant.

POST /requesttopay avec X-Reference-Id = 11377cbe-374c-43f6-a019-4fb70e57b617

GET /requesttopay/11377cbe-374c-43f6-a019-4fb70e57b617 retourne le statut de la requete.

PUT
La methode PUT est utilisee par l'Open API lors de l'envoi des callbacks. Un callback est envoye si une URL de callback est incluse dans la requete POST. La plateforme Wallet envoie le callback une seule fois. Il n'y a pas de retry si le systeme Partner ne repond pas. Si le callback n'est pas recu, le systeme Partner peut utiliser GET pour verifier le statut.
